title: Workplace Communication
description: Advanced vocabulary and expressions for meetings, projects, collaboration, and professional communication.

cards.definition_en: A system for sending digital messages electronically over the internet.
cards.example_sentence: I sent you an email yesterday.
cards.example_es: Te envié un correo electrónico ayer.
cards.example_en: I sent you an email yesterday.

cards.definition_en: The line of text that identifies the topic of an email or message.
cards.example_sentence: In the email, the subject line says "Meeting tomorrow".
cards.example_es: En el correo, el asunto dice "Reunión mañana".
cards.example_en: In the email, the subject line says "Meeting tomorrow".

cards.definition_en: A supplementary item, such as a file or document, that is attached to a message or object.
cards.example_sentence: Please find the attachment with the report you requested.
cards.example_es: Adjunto el informe que solicitaste.
cards.example_en: Please find the attachment with the report you requested.

cards.definition_en: To send something on to another recipient.
cards.example_sentence: Can you forward the email to all team members?
cards.example_es: ¿Puedes reenviar el correo a todos los miembros del equipo?
cards.example_en: Can you forward the email to all team members?

cards.definition_en: Send a response to all recipients of an email.
cards.example_sentence: Remember to reply all to the group's messages.
cards.example_es: Recuerda responder a todos los mensajes del grupo.
cards.example_en: Remember to reply all to the group's messages.

cards.definition_en: An action taken to continue or check the progress of something previously started.
cards.example_sentence: I will do a follow-up on the project next week.
cards.example_es: Haré un seguimiento del proyecto la próxima semana.
cards.example_en: I will do a follow-up on the project next week.

cards.definition_en: To arrange a time for a meeting.
cards.example_sentence: I have to schedule a meeting with the client tomorrow.
cards.example_es: Tengo que programar una reunión con el cliente mañana.
cards.example_en: I have to schedule a meeting with the client tomorrow.

cards.definition_en: To delay or put off something to a later time.
cards.example_sentence: We decided to postpone the meeting until next week.
cards.example_es: Decidimos aplazar la reunión para la próxima semana.
cards.example_en: We decided to postpone the meeting until next week.

cards.definition_en: To arrange for something to occur at a different time.
cards.example_sentence: I will reschedule the meeting for Friday.
cards.example_es: Voy a reprogramar la reunión para el viernes.
cards.example_en: I will reschedule the meeting for Friday.

cards.definition_en: A list of items to be discussed or acted upon, especially at a meeting.
cards.example_sentence: I checked my agenda before the meeting.
cards.example_es: Revisé mi agenda antes de la reunión.
cards.example_en: I checked my agenda before the meeting.

cards.definition_en: A specific topic scheduled for discussion on an agenda.
cards.example_sentence: The next agenda item will be the approval of the budget.
cards.example_es: El siguiente punto del orden del día será la aprobación del presupuesto.
cards.example_en: The next agenda item will be the approval of the budget.

cards.definition_en: A written record of the discussions and decisions made during a meeting.
cards.example_sentence: The minutes of the meeting were approved.
cards.example_es: El acta de la reunión se aprobó.
cards.example_en: The minutes of the meeting were approved.

cards.definition_en: A specific task or piece of work that needs to be completed.
cards.example_sentence: The action item for the next meeting is to review the budget.
cards.example_es: El punto de acción para la próxima reunión es revisar el presupuesto.
cards.example_en: The action item for the next meeting is to review the budget.

cards.definition_en: A date or time by which something must be completed or submitted.
cards.example_sentence: The deadline to submit the report is Monday at 5 p.m.
cards.example_es: La fecha límite para entregar el informe es el lunes a las 5 p.m.
cards.example_en: The deadline to submit the report is Monday at 5 p.m.

cards.definition_en: A significant event or point in development that marks progress or a turning point.
cards.example_sentence: This project reached a major milestone when we completed the testing phase.
cards.example_es: Este proyecto alcanzó un hito importante cuando completamos la fase de pruebas.
cards.example_en: This project reached a major milestone when we completed the testing phase.

cards.definition_en: A tangible or intangible item that must be completed and handed over as part of a project or contract.
cards.example_sentence: The deliverable must be submitted before Friday.
cards.example_es: El entregable debe enviarse antes del viernes.
cards.example_en: The deliverable must be submitted before Friday.

cards.definition_en: The amount of work assigned to or expected from a person or group.
cards.example_sentence: My workload has increased a lot this month.
cards.example_es: Mi carga de trabajo ha aumentado mucho este mes.
cards.example_en: My workload has increased a lot this month.

cards.definition_en: To arrange or deal with tasks, items, or goals in order of importance, giving precedence to the most important ones.
cards.example_sentence: It's important to prioritize the most urgent tasks before starting the project.
cards.example_es: Es importante priorizar las tareas más urgentes antes de comenzar el proyecto.
cards.example_en: It's important to prioritize the most urgent tasks before starting the project.

cards.definition_en: To raise an issue to a higher level of authority or attention.
cards.example_sentence: If it isn't resolved quickly, we will have to escalate an issue to the support team.
cards.example_es: Si no se resuelve rápidamente, tendremos que escalar un problema al equipo de soporte.
cards.example_en: If it isn't resolved quickly, we will have to escalate an issue to the support team.

cards.definition_en: To find a solution to a problem or difficulty.
main_translations_es: Encontrar una solución a un problema
collocations: resolve an issue with the team
cards.example_sentence: I need to resolve a problem before the meeting.
cards.example_es: Necesito resolver un problema antes de la reunión.
cards.example_en: I need to resolve a problem before the meeting.

cards.definition_en: To reach the same level or position as someone else after having fallen behind.
cards.example_sentence: After the holidays, I had to catch up with work.
cards.example_es: Después de las vacaciones, tuve que ponerme al día con el trabajo.
cards.example_en: After the holidays, I had to catch up with work.

cards.definition_en: A document that details the status and developments of a project or task.
cards.example_sentence: The team submitted the progress report before the deadline.
cards.example_es: El equipo entregó el informe de progreso antes de la fecha límite.
cards.example_en: The team submitted the progress report before the deadline.

cards.definition_en: The present condition or situation of something.
cards.example_sentence: The current status of the project is uncertain.
cards.example_es: El estado actual del proyecto es incierto.
cards.example_en: The current status of the project is uncertain.

cards.definition_en: Later than planned or expected; not on time.
cards.example_sentence: The project is behind schedule and will not be finished on time.
cards.example_es: El proyecto está retrasado y no se completará a tiempo.
cards.example_en: The project is behind schedule and will not be finished on time.

cards.definition_en: Occurring earlier than the planned time.
cards.example_sentence: The project is ahead of schedule and we will finish earlier than planned.
cards.example_es: El proyecto está adelantado y terminaremos antes de lo previsto.
cards.example_en: The project is ahead of schedule and we will finish earlier than planned.

cards.definition_en: To complete something before or at the specified time limit.
cards.example_sentence: We have to meet the deadline to submit the report.
cards.example_es: Tenemos que cumplir el plazo para presentar el informe.
cards.example_en: We have to meet the deadline to submit the report.

cards.definition_en: To fail to meet a deadline.
cards.example_sentence: I don't want to miss the deadline for submission.
cards.example_es: No quiero incumplir el plazo de entrega.
cards.example_en: I don't want to miss the deadline for submission.

cards.definition_en: The state of being ready for use or obtainable.
cards.example_sentence: The availability of seats is limited.
cards.example_es: La disponibilidad de los asientos es limitada.
cards.example_en: The availability of seats is limited.

cards.definition_en: A specific period of time allocated for an activity or appointment.
cards.example_sentence: What is your available time slot for the meeting?
cards.example_es: ¿Cuál es tu franja horaria disponible para la reunión?
cards.example_en: What is your available time slot for the meeting?

cards.definition_en: A situation where two or more events are planned for the same time, making it impossible to attend all of them.
cards.example_sentence: I have a scheduling conflict and won't be able to attend the meeting.
cards.example_es: Tengo un conflicto de horario y no podré asistir a la reunión.
cards.example_en: I have a scheduling conflict and won't be able to attend the meeting.

cards.definition_en: A pre-designed format or pattern used as a starting point for creating something.
cards.example_sentence: The template of the report is available on the intranet.
cards.example_es: La plantilla del informe está disponible en la intranet.
cards.example_en: The template of the report is available on the intranet.

cards.definition_en: A preliminary version of a document or plan.
cards.example_sentence: I will review the draft of the report before sending it.
cards.example_es: Voy a revisar el borrador del informe antes de enviarlo.
cards.example_en: I will review the draft of the report before sending it.

cards.definition_en: A plan that estimates expected income and expenses over a specific period.
cards.example_sentence: The budget for the project was approved last week.
cards.example_es: El presupuesto para el proyecto se aprobó la semana pasada.
cards.example_en: The budget for the project was approved last week.

cards.definition_en: A prediction or estimate of future events, such as weather or economic conditions.
cards.example_sentence: The forecast predicts rain for tomorrow.
cards.example_es: La previsión del tiempo indica lluvia para mañana.
cards.example_en: The forecast predicts rain for tomorrow.

cards.definition_en: An approximate calculation or judgment of the value, size, or amount of something.
cards.example_sentence: According to the meteorologist's estimate, it will rain tomorrow.
cards.example_es: Según la estimación del meteorólogo, lloverá mañana.
cards.example_en: According to the meteorologist's estimate, it will rain tomorrow.

cards.definition_en: the extent or range of something's coverage, influence, or operation
cards.example_sentence: The scope of the project includes the implementation of new technologies.
cards.example_es: El alcance del proyecto incluye la implementación de nuevas tecnologías.
cards.example_en: The scope of the project includes the implementation of new technologies.

cards.definition_en: Something that is needed or demanded.
cards.example_sentence: The requirement to enroll in the course is to be at least 18 years old.
cards.example_es: El requisito para inscribirse en el curso es tener al menos 18 años.
cards.example_en: The requirement to enroll in the course is to be at least 18 years old.

cards.definition_en: A condition in which a person, group, or thing relies on or is controlled by another for support or aid.
cards.example_sentence: The dependency on fossil fuels is an environmental problem.
cards.example_es: La dependencia de los combustibles fósiles es un problema ambiental.
cards.example_en: The dependency on fossil fuels is an environmental problem.

cards.definition_en: A narrow point or stage that restricts flow or progress, causing a delay.
cards.example_sentence: The project was delayed because there was a bottleneck in production.
cards.example_es: El proyecto se retrasó porque hubo un cuello de botella en la producción.
cards.example_en: The project was delayed because there was a bottleneck in production.

cards.definition_en: the possibility of loss, injury, or other adverse outcome
cards.example_sentence: Investing in that company carries a great risk.
cards.example_es: Invertir en esa empresa conlleva un gran riesgo.
cards.example_en: Investing in that company carries a great risk.

cards.definition_en: The act of reducing the severity, seriousness, or harmful effects of something.
cards.example_sentence: Mitigation of climate change requires urgent actions.
cards.example_es: La mitigación del cambio climático requiere acciones urgentes.
cards.example_en: Mitigation of climate change requires urgent actions.

cards.definition_en: The act of sending a product, vehicle, or spacecraft into operation or into the air.
cards.example_sentence: The launch of the new phone will be next month.
cards.example_es: El lanzamiento del nuevo teléfono será el próximo mes.
cards.example_en: The launch of the new phone will be next month.

cards.definition_en: The act of introducing or launching a new product, service, or system to the market or users.
cards.example_sentence: The rollout of the new software is scheduled for next Monday.
cards.example_es: El despliegue del nuevo software está programado para el próximo lunes.
cards.example_en: The rollout of the new software is scheduled for next Monday.

cards.definition_en: The process of integrating new employees into an organization.
cards.example_sentence: The onboarding of the new employees will take place next week.
cards.example_es: La incorporación de los nuevos empleados se realizará la próxima semana.
cards.example_en: The onboarding of the new employees will take place next week.

cards.definition_en: The process of teaching or developing skills and knowledge.
cards.example_sentence: Staff training is essential to improve productivity.
cards.example_es: La capacitación del personal es esencial para mejorar la productividad.
cards.example_en: Staff training is essential to improve productivity.

cards.definition_en: Information or reactions given about a performance, product, or behavior, used to guide improvement.
cards.example_sentence: User feedback is essential for improving the product.
cards.example_es: La retroalimentación de los usuarios es esencial para mejorar el producto.
cards.example_en: User feedback is essential for improving the product.

cards.definition_en: A formal assessment of an employee's work performance.
cards.example_sentence: The performance review will take place next Monday.
cards.example_es: La evaluación de desempeño se realizará el próximo lunes.
cards.example_en: The performance review will take place next Monday.

cards.definition_en: A promotion is the advancement of an employee to a higher position, often with increased responsibilities and pay.
cards.example_sentence: After three years of hard work, he received a promotion.
cards.example_es: Después de tres años de trabajo duro, recibió un ascenso.
cards.example_en: After three years of hard work, he received a promotion.

cards.definition_en: The act of voluntarily giving up a job or position.
cards.example_sentence: His resignation from the position surprised everyone.
cards.example_es: Su renuncia al cargo sorprendió a todos.
cards.example_en: His resignation from the position surprised everyone.

cards.definition_en: The act of employing someone for a job.
cards.example_sentence: Hiring new employees is a priority this quarter.
cards.example_es: La contratación de nuevos empleados es una prioridad este trimestre.
cards.example_en: Hiring new employees is a priority this quarter.

cards.definition_en: A formal meeting where one person asks questions and another provides answers, often to assess suitability for a job or to gather information.
cards.example_sentence: Tomorrow I have a job interview at ten o'clock.
cards.example_es: Mañana tengo una entrevista de trabajo a las diez.
cards.example_en: Tomorrow I have a job interview at ten o'clock.

cards.definition_en: A person who is seeking or being considered for a position, office, or honor.
cards.example_sentence: The candidate won the election with a large majority.
cards.example_es: El candidato ganó las elecciones con una amplia mayoría.
cards.example_en: The candidate won the election with a large majority.

cards.definition_en: A position or opportunity that is available, especially a job vacancy.
cards.example_sentence: There is an opening for a software engineer at the company.
cards.example_es: Hay una vacante para ingeniero de software en la empresa.
cards.example_en: There is an opening for a software engineer at the company.

cards.definition_en: A written statement outlining the duties, responsibilities, and requirements of a particular job.
cards.example_sentence: The job description includes project management responsibilities.
cards.example_es: La descripción del puesto incluye responsabilidades de gestión de proyectos.
cards.example_en: The job description includes project management responsibilities.

cards.definition_en: A work arrangement in which an employee performs their job duties from a location outside the employer's premises, typically using digital communication tools.
cards.example_sentence: I work remotely from home every day.
cards.example_es: Trabajo remoto desde casa todos los días.
cards.example_en: I work remotely from home every day.

cards.definition_en: A work arrangement that combines remote and on‑site duties.
cards.example_sentence: I prefer hybrid work because it combines office and remote work.
cards.example_es: Prefiero el trabajo híbrido porque combina la oficina y el teletrabajo.
cards.example_en: I prefer hybrid work because it combines office and remote work.

cards.definition_en: Located or occurring at the place where the activity takes place.
cards.example_sentence: The course will be on-site, so you'll have to attend in person.
cards.example_es: El curso será presencial, así que tendrás que asistir en persona.
cards.example_en: The course will be on-site, so you'll have to attend in person.

cards.definition_en: A person who works with you, typically in the same organization or workplace.
cards.example_sentence: My coworker helped me finish the report.
cards.example_es: Mi compañero de trabajo me ayudó a terminar el informe.
cards.example_en: My coworker helped me finish the report.

cards.definition_en: A person or group that has an interest or concern in something, especially a business or project.
cards.example_sentence: The stakeholder approved the new project plan.
cards.example_es: La parte interesada aprobó el nuevo plan del proyecto.
cards.example_en: The stakeholder approved the new project plan.

cards.definition_en: A person or organization that receives professional services or purchases goods from a business.
cards.example_sentence: The client requested a refund.
cards.example_es: El cliente pidió una devolución.
cards.example_en: The client requested a refund.

cards.definition_en: A person or company that sells goods or services.
cards.example_sentence: The vendor delivered the goods on time.
cards.example_es: El proveedor entregó la mercancía a tiempo.
cards.example_en: The vendor delivered the goods on time.

cards.definition_en: To discuss terms and reach an agreement.
cards.example_sentence: We will negotiate the terms of the contract tomorrow.
cards.example_es: Vamos a negociar los términos del contrato mañana.
cards.example_en: We will negotiate the terms of the contract tomorrow.

cards.definition_en: To arrive at a shared agreement among all parties.
cards.example_sentence: After long discussions, we finally managed to reach a consensus on the project.
cards.example_es: Después de largas discusiones, finalmente logramos llegar a un consenso sobre el proyecto.
cards.example_en: After long discussions, we finally managed to reach a consensus on the project.

cards.definition_en: to agree upon or adopt a common position or plan
cards.example_sentence: The teams must align on the company's objectives.
cards.example_es: Los equipos deben alinearse con los objetivos de la empresa.
cards.example_en: The teams must align on the company's objectives.

cards.definition_en: To make the expectations clear.
cards.example_sentence: It's important to clarify expectations before starting the project.
cards.example_es: Es importante aclarar expectativas antes de iniciar el proyecto.
cards.example_en: It's important to clarify expectations before starting the project.

cards.definition_en: To accept responsibility for something.
cards.example_sentence: It's important to take ownership of your mistakes.
cards.example_es: Es importante asumir responsabilidad por tus errores.
cards.example_en: It's important to take ownership of your mistakes.

cards.definition_en: To entrust a task or responsibility to another person.
cards.example_sentence: I learned to delegate tasks to be more efficient.
cards.example_es: Aprendí a delegar tareas para ser más eficiente.
cards.example_en: I learned to delegate tasks to be more efficient.

cards.definition_en: To give responsibility for a task to someone else.
collocations: hand over a task to a colleague
cards.example_sentence: I will hand over a task to my colleague so they can finish it sooner.
cards.example_es: Voy a transferir una tarea a mi colega para que la termine antes.
cards.example_en: I will hand over a task to my colleague so they can finish it sooner.

cards.definition_en: To officially accept or give consent to something.
cards.example_sentence: The committee decided to approve the new policy.
cards.example_es: El comité decidió aprobar la nueva política.
cards.example_en: The committee decided to approve the new policy.

cards.definition_en: To refuse to accept, consider, or agree with something.
cards.example_sentence: The committee decided to reject the proposed tariff increase.
cards.example_es: El comité decidió rechazar la propuesta de aumento de tarifas.
cards.example_en: The committee decided to reject the proposed tariff increase.

cards.definition_en: To examine or assess something again or in detail.
cards.example_sentence: I will review the report before sending it.
cards.example_es: Voy a revisar el informe antes de enviarlo.
cards.example_en: I will review the report before sending it.

cards.definition_en: To prepare a preliminary version of a document or text.
cards.example_sentence: I will draft the report before the meeting.
cards.example_es: Voy a redactar el informe antes de la reunión.
cards.example_en: I will draft the report before the meeting.

cards.definition_en: To give a brief statement of the main points of something.
cards.example_sentence: Please summarize the article in three sentences.
cards.example_es: Por favor, resume el artículo en tres frases.
cards.example_en: Please summarize the article in three sentences.

cards.definition_en: To emphasize or make something stand out.
cards.example_sentence: The report should highlight last year's achievements.
cards.example_es: El informe debe destacar los logros del año pasado.
cards.example_en: The report should highlight last year's achievements.

cards.definition_en: To bring up or express a worry or issue.
cards.example_sentence: I wanted to raise a concern about the new work schedule.
cards.example_es: Quería plantear una inquietud sobre el nuevo horario de trabajo.
cards.example_en: I wanted to raise a concern about the new work schedule.

cards.definition_en: To deal with or discuss a problem or topic.
collocations: address an issue in the meeting
cards.example_sentence: We need to address the issue of safety in the company as soon as possible.
cards.example_es: Necesitamos abordar el asunto de la seguridad en la empresa lo antes posible.
cards.example_en: We need to address the issue of safety in the company as soon as possible.

cards.definition_en: To continue an action or plan until it is completed.
cards.example_sentence: It's important to follow through with patient follow-up after surgery.
cards.example_es: Es importante cumplir con el seguimiento de los pacientes después de la cirugía.
cards.example_en: It's important to follow through with patient follow-up after surgery.

cards.definition_en: To complete a process by providing the missing information or final step.
collocations: close the loop in the process
cards.example_sentence: To prevent a short circuit, we must close the loop before turning on the device.
cards.example_es: Para evitar el cortocircuito, debemos cerrar el circuito antes de encender el dispositivo.
cards.example_en: To prevent a short circuit, we must close the loop before turning on the device.

cards.definition_en: To keep someone informed about developments.
cards.example_sentence: Please keep the customers posted about the schedule changes.
cards.example_es: Por favor, mantén a los clientes informados sobre los cambios en el horario.
cards.example_en: Please keep the customers posted about the schedule changes.

cards.definition_en: A series of related email messages that share the same subject line, showing the progression of a conversation.
cards.example_sentence: I reviewed the email thread to find the information I needed.
cards.example_es: Revisé el hilo de correo para encontrar la información que necesitaba.
cards.example_en: I reviewed the email thread to find the information I needed.

cards.definition_en: A brief telephone conversation.
cards.example_sentence: I'll make a quick call to confirm the time.
cards.example_es: Haré una llamada breve para confirmar la hora.
cards.example_en: I'll make a quick call to confirm the time.

cards.definition_en: A private meeting between two individuals, often a manager and an employee, conducted face‑to‑face.
cards.example_sentence: I have a one-on-one meeting with my supervisor tomorrow at ten.
cards.example_es: Tengo una reunión individual con mi supervisor mañana a las diez.
cards.example_en: I have a one-on-one meeting with my supervisor tomorrow at ten.

cards.definition_en: A meeting where participants generate ideas and solutions through spontaneous discussion.
cards.example_sentence: Tomorrow we will have a brainstorming session to improve the product.
cards.example_es: Mañana tendremos una sesión de lluvia de ideas para mejorar el producto.
cards.example_en: Tomorrow we will have a brainstorming session to improve the product.

cards.definition_en: A formal introduction or display of information, such as a talk, demonstration, or visual material.
cards.example_sentence: The presentation of the project was very clear.
cards.example_es: La presentación del proyecto fue muy clara.
cards.example_en: The presentation of the project was very clear.

cards.definition_en: A single page of a presentation, typically shown on a screen.
cards.example_sentence: The slide shows the results of the experiment.
cards.example_es: La diapositiva muestra los resultados del experimento.
cards.example_en: The slide shows the results of the experiment.

cards.definition_en: A plan or suggestion put forward for consideration or discussion.
cards.example_sentence: The committee's proposal was approved unanimously.
cards.example_es: La propuesta del comité fue aprobada por unanimidad.
cards.example_en: The committee's proposal was approved unanimously.

cards.definition_en: A document itemizing goods or services provided and requesting payment.
cards.example_sentence: I need you to send me the invoice before Friday.
cards.example_es: Necesito que me envíes la factura antes del viernes.
cards.example_en: I need you to send me the invoice before Friday.

cards.definition_en: A document issued by a buyer to a seller specifying the items, quantities, and agreed prices for a purchase.
cards.example_sentence: I need to send the purchase order to the supplier before Friday.
cards.example_es: Necesito enviar la orden de compra al proveedor antes del viernes.
cards.example_en: I need to send the purchase order to the supplier before Friday.

cards.definition_en: A repayment of money that has been spent or lost, typically to cover incurred expenses.
cards.example_sentence: I requested the reimbursement for my purchase because the product was defective.
cards.example_es: Solicité el reembolso de mi compra porque el producto estaba defectuoso.
cards.example_en: I requested the reimbursement for my purchase because the product was defective.

cards.definition_en: A period of time when an employee is permitted to be absent from work due to illness.
cards.example_sentence: After the surgery, he needed a sick leave of two weeks.
cards.example_es: Después de la operación, necesitó una baja médica de dos semanas.
cards.example_en: After the surgery, he needed a sick leave of two weeks.

cards.definition_en: Paid time off from work that an employee is entitled to each year.
cards.example_sentence: I am going to take my annual leave next week.
cards.example_es: Voy a tomar mis vacaciones la próxima semana.
cards.example_en: I am going to take my annual leave next week.

cards.definition_en: A period of time when someone is permitted to be away from work or school.
collocations: apply for a leave of absence
cards.example_sentence: I requested a leave of absence to take care of my family.
cards.example_es: Solicité un permiso de ausencia para poder atender a mi familia.
cards.example_en: I requested a leave of absence to take care of my family.

cards.definition_en: A designated span of time during which an employee's performance is evaluated before confirming permanent employment.
cards.example_sentence: The new employee will start his probation period next week.
cards.example_es: El nuevo empleado comenzará su período de prueba la próxima semana.
cards.example_en: The new employee will start his probation period next week.

cards.definition_en: A target or objective set to be achieved within a three-month period.
cards.example_sentence: Our quarterly goal is to increase sales by 10%.
cards.example_es: Nuestro objetivo trimestral es aumentar las ventas en un 10 %.
cards.example_en: Our quarterly goal is to increase sales by 10%.

cards.definition_en: A measurable value that shows how effectively an organization is achieving its key objectives.
cards.example_sentence: The company's key performance indicator shows a 15% improvement in productivity.
cards.example_es: El indicador clave de rendimiento de la empresa muestra una mejora del 15% en la productividad.
cards.example_en: The company's key performance indicator shows a 15% improvement in productivity.

cards.definition_en: A result that can be quantified or assessed.
cards.example_sentence: Measurable outcome is essential to evaluate the project's success.
cards.example_es: Resultado medible es esencial para evaluar el éxito del proyecto.
cards.example_en: Measurable outcome is essential to evaluate the project's success.

cards.definition_en: The process of developing skills, knowledge, and experience in one's career.
cards.example_sentence: Professional growth depends on continuous training.
cards.example_es: El crecimiento profesional depende de la capacitación continua.
cards.example_en: Professional growth depends on continuous training.

cards.definition_en: The process of identifying and choosing among alternatives.
cards.example_sentence: Decision-making is crucial in project management.
cards.example_es: La toma de decisiones es crucial en la gestión de proyectos.
cards.example_en: Decision-making is crucial in project management.

cards.definition_en: To give official permission or authority to someone to perform an action.
cards.example_sentence: The manager must authorize the expense before it is made.
cards.example_es: El gerente debe autorizar el gasto antes de que se realice.
cards.example_en: The manager must authorize the expense before it is made.

cards.definition_en: To distribute or assign resources to particular tasks or purposes.
cards.example_sentence: The manager decided to allocate resources to the research project.
cards.example_es: El gerente decidió asignar recursos al proyecto de investigación.
cards.example_en: The manager decided to allocate resources to the research project.

cards.definition_en: To continue doing something in the correct direction or according to a plan.
collocations: stay on track with your goals
stay on track during the meeting
stay on track and avoid setbacks
cards.example_sentence: To finish the project on time, we need to stay on track and not deviate.
cards.example_es: Para terminar el proyecto a tiempo, debemos mantener el rumbo y no desviarnos.
cards.example_en: To finish the project on time, we need to stay on track and not deviate.

cards.definition_en: To deviate from the intended plan or course.
collocations: go off track during a meeting
go off track with the project
cards.example_sentence: We must not go off track.
cards.example_es: No debemos desviarnos del plan.
cards.example_en: We must not go off track.

cards.definition_en: Requires further review or correction.
cards.example_sentence: The report needs revision before sending it.
cards.example_es: El informe necesita revisión antes de enviarlo.
cards.example_en: The report needs revision before sending it.

cards.definition_en: requiring immediate attention or action
cards.example_sentence: I need you to send me the report as soon as possible; it's urgent.
cards.example_es: Necesito que me envíes el informe lo antes posible, es urgente.
cards.example_en: I need you to send me the report as soon as possible; it's urgent.

cards.definition_en: Something that is considered very important and should be dealt with before other matters.
cards.example_sentence: This project is of high priority for the company.
cards.example_es: Este proyecto es de alta prioridad para la empresa.
cards.example_en: This project is of high priority for the company.

cards.definition_en: Something that is considered less important or urgent than other items.
cards.example_sentence: This ticket has low priority, so we can address it later.
cards.example_es: Este ticket tiene baja prioridad, así que podemos atenderlo más tarde.
cards.example_en: This ticket has low priority, so we can address it later.

cards.definition_en: A person or place that serves as the primary source of communication or information.
cards.example_sentence: For any questions, please contact Maria, who will be your point of contact.
cards.example_es: Para cualquier duda, por favor contacte a María, quien será su punto de contacto.
cards.example_en: For any questions, please contact Maria, who will be your point of contact.

cards.definition_en: To display one's computer screen to others, typically during a video call or presentation.
collocations: share the screen with the team
share the screen during the meeting
share the screen for a presentation
cards.example_sentence: I need to share the screen during the meeting.
cards.example_es: Necesito compartir pantalla durante la reunión.
cards.example_en: I need to share the screen during the meeting.

cards.definition_en: To turn off or lower the microphone's sound so it cannot be heard.
collocations: He forgot to mute the microphone
cards.example_sentence: You can mute the microphone if you don't want to be heard.
cards.example_es: Puedes silenciar el micrófono si no quieres que te escuchen.
cards.example_en: You can mute the microphone if you don't want to be heard.

cards.definition_en: To connect to or participate in a scheduled meeting.
cards.example_sentence: I need to join the meeting at ten in the morning.
cards.example_es: Necesito conectarme a la reunión a las diez de la mañana.
cards.example_en: I need to join the meeting at ten in the morning.

cards.definition_en: To exit or depart from a meeting.
collocations: to leave the meeting without notice
cards.example_sentence: I'm going to leave the meeting because it's getting late.
cards.example_es: Voy a abandonar la reunión porque ya es tarde.
cards.example_en: I'm going to leave the meeting because it's getting late.

cards.definition_en: To write down information for later reference.
cards.example_sentence: During the class, you should take notes of everything the teacher says.
cards.example_es: Durante la clase, debes tomar nota de todo lo que el profesor dice.
cards.example_en: During the class, you should take notes of everything the teacher says.

cards.definition_en: To present a demonstration of something, typically showing how it works.
collocations: give a demo of the product
give a demo to the client
give a demo during the meeting
cards.example_sentence: I'm going to give a demo of the new software to the team.
cards.example_es: Voy a hacer una demostración del nuevo software para el equipo.
cards.example_en: I'm going to give a demo of the new software to the team.

cards.definition_en: A set of actions or tasks that should be taken after the current point.
cards.example_sentence: The next steps are to review the report and present the results.
cards.example_es: Los próximos pasos son revisar el informe y presentar los resultados.
cards.example_en: The next steps are to review the report and present the results.

cards.definition_en: A continuation or prolongation of something, especially a period of time.
cards.example_sentence: The referee granted a five‑minute extension to the game.
cards.example_es: El árbitro concedió una prórroga de cinco minutos al partido.
cards.example_en: The referee granted a five‑minute extension to the game.

cards.definition_en: A method for overcoming a problem or limitation in a system.
cards.example_sentence: We implemented a workaround while the team fixes the bug.
cards.example_es: Implementamos una solución alternativa mientras el equipo corrige el error.
cards.example_en: We implemented a workaround while the team fixes the bug.

cards.definition_en: A person or thing that prevents progress or blocks something.
cards.example_sentence: He installed an ad blocker to avoid intrusive ads.
cards.example_es: Instaló un bloqueador de anuncios para evitar la publicidad intrusiva.
cards.example_en: He installed an ad blocker to avoid intrusive ads.

cards.definition_en: The state of being ready for use right away.
cards.example_sentence: I have immediate availability to start working.
cards.example_es: Tengo disponibilidad inmediata para comenzar a trabajar.
cards.example_en: I have immediate availability to start working.

cards.definition_en: A setting or system used to conduct testing of software or processes.
cards.example_sentence: The team set up the test environment before releasing the new version.
cards.example_es: El equipo configuró el entorno de pruebas antes de lanzar la nueva versión.
cards.example_en: The team set up the test environment before releasing the new version.

cards.definition_en: The setting where software or a system is operated for real users, as opposed to testing or development.
cards.example_sentence: The software was deployed to the production environment after testing.
cards.example_es: El software se desplegó en el entorno de producción después de las pruebas.
cards.example_en: The software was deployed to the production environment after testing.

cards.definition_en: A sequence of individuals or steps required to give approval for a decision or document.
cards.example_sentence: For the project, we follow an approval chain before launching the product.
cards.example_es: Para el proyecto, seguimos una cadena de aprobación antes de lanzar el producto.
cards.example_en: For the project, we follow an approval chain before launching the product.

title: Academic English
description: Upper-intermediate and advanced academic vocabulary for essays, reports, presentations, and formal discussion.

cards.definition_en: A proposed explanation for a phenomenon that can be tested through observation or experiment.
cards.example_sentence: My hypothesis is that the rise in temperature affects bird migration.
cards.example_es: Mi hipótesis es que el aumento de la temperatura afecta la migración de las aves.
cards.example_en: My hypothesis is that the rise in temperature affects bird migration.

cards.definition_en: A symbol or quantity that can change or take on different values.
cards.example_sentence: The temperature is variable in this region.
cards.example_es: La temperatura es variable en esta región.
cards.example_en: The temperature is variable in this region.

cards.definition_en: A systematic set of methods and principles used in a particular field of study or practice.
cards.example_sentence: The methodology the researcher used ensured reliable results.
cards.example_es: La metodología que utilizó el investigador garantizó resultados fiables.
cards.example_en: The methodology the researcher used ensured reliable results.

cards.definition_en: A comprehensive summary and analysis of existing research and publications on a particular topic.
cards.example_sentence: For my thesis, I conducted an exhaustive literature review of recent studies.
cards.example_es: Para mi tesis, realicé una revisión bibliográfica exhaustiva de los estudios recientes.
cards.example_en: For my thesis, I conducted an exhaustive literature review of recent studies.

cards.definition_en: A process in which experts evaluate each other's work, such as academic articles, to ensure quality and validity.
cards.example_sentence: Peer review is essential to ensure the quality of scientific articles.
cards.example_es: La revisión por pares es esencial para garantizar la calidad de los artículos científicos.
cards.example_en: Peer review is essential to ensure the quality of scientific articles.

cards.definition_en: Based on observation or experience rather than theory or pure logic.
cards.example_sentence: The empirical method relies on direct observation of phenomena.
cards.example_es: El método empírico se basa en la observación directa de los fenómenos.
cards.example_en: The empirical method relies on direct observation of phenomena.

cards.definition_en: relating to the qualities or characteristics of something rather than its quantity
cards.example_sentence: The study used a qualitative approach to understand patients' experiences.
cards.example_es: El estudio utilizó un enfoque cualitativo para comprender las experiencias de los pacientes.
cards.example_en: The study used a qualitative approach to understand patients' experiences.

cards.definition_en: Relating to, measuring, or expressed in terms of quantity.
cards.example_sentence: The quantitative analysis showed a 15% increase in sales.
cards.example_es: El análisis cuantitativo mostró un aumento del 15% en las ventas.
cards.example_en: The quantitative analysis showed a 15% increase in sales.

cards.definition_en: The number of observations or items included in a statistical sample.
cards.example_sentence: The sample size must be large enough to obtain reliable results.
cards.example_es: El tamaño de la muestra debe ser suficientemente grande para obtener resultados fiables.
cards.example_en: The sample size must be large enough to obtain reliable results.

cards.definition_en: Results or conclusions drawn from an investigation or study.
cards.example_sentence: The findings of the study suggest that diet affects cardiovascular health.
cards.example_es: Los hallazgos del estudio sugieren que la dieta afecta la salud cardiovascular.
cards.example_en: The findings of the study suggest that diet affects cardiovascular health.

cards.definition_en: The possible results or effects that may follow from an action, decision, or event.
cards.example_sentence: The implications of his decision will be felt throughout the company.
cards.example_es: Las implicaciones de su decisión se sentirán en toda la empresa.
cards.example_en: The implications of his decision will be felt throughout the company.

cards.definition_en: Restrictions or constraints that limit what can be done or achieved.
cards.example_sentence: The limitations of the new software are evident in its performance.
cards.example_es: Las limitaciones del nuevo software son evidentes en su rendimiento.
cards.example_en: The limitations of the new software are evident in its performance.

cards.definition_en: A set of concepts and theories that provides a basis for understanding and analyzing a particular research topic.
cards.example_sentence: The theoretical framework of the study is based on contemporary sociological theories.
cards.example_es: El marco teórico del estudio se basa en teorías sociológicas contemporáneas.
cards.example_en: The theoretical framework of the study is based on contemporary sociological theories.

cards.definition_en: Research that has been conducted earlier on a given topic.
cards.example_sentence: Previous research suggests that the method is effective.
cards.example_es: La investigación previa sugiere que el método es efectivo.
cards.example_en: Previous research suggests that the method is effective.

cards.definition_en: A collection of facts, testimony, or other information that together support a particular conclusion or argument.
collocations: the body of evidence points to
the body of evidence is compelling
cards.example_sentence: The body of evidence presented at the trial was sufficient to convict the defendant.
cards.example_es: El cuerpo de evidencia presentado en el juicio fue suficiente para condenar al acusado.
cards.example_en: The body of evidence presented at the trial was sufficient to convict the defendant.

cards.definition_en: A collection of data, typically organized for analysis or reference.
cards.example_sentence: The dataset contains information about household energy consumption.
cards.example_es: El conjunto de datos contiene información sobre el consumo energético de los hogares.
cards.example_en: The dataset contains information about household energy consumption.

cards.definition_en: To explain the meaning of something, or to understand and convey it in a particular way.
cards.example_sentence: The translator must interpret the text accurately.
cards.example_es: El traductor debe interpretar el texto con precisión.
cards.example_en: The translator must interpret the text accurately.

cards.definition_en: To examine methodically by breaking something into its components and studying their relationships.
cards.example_sentence: Scientists must analyze the data before publishing their conclusions.
cards.example_es: Los científicos deben analizar los datos antes de publicar sus conclusiones.
cards.example_en: Scientists must analyze the data before publishing their conclusions.

cards.definition_en: To combine different ideas, information, or substances into a unified whole.
cards.example_sentence: The chemist managed to synthesize a new molecule in the lab.
cards.example_es: El químico logró sintetizar una nueva molécula en el laboratorio.
cards.example_en: The chemist managed to synthesize a new molecule in the lab.

cards.definition_en: To assess or judge the value, quality, or significance of something.
cards.example_sentence: The committee must evaluate the experiment's results before publishing the report.
cards.example_es: El comité debe evaluar los resultados del experimento antes de publicar el informe.
cards.example_en: The committee must evaluate the experiment's results before publishing the report.

cards.definition_en: A detailed analysis and assessment of something, especially a work of art, literature, or performance.
cards.example_sentence: The critic decided to critique the new movie for its lack of originality.
cards.example_es: El crítico decidió criticar la nueva película por su falta de originalidad.
cards.example_en: The critic decided to critique the new movie for its lack of originality.

cards.definition_en: To give reasons for or against something, often in a discussion or debate.
cards.example_sentence: It's important to know how to argue your ideas during the debate.
cards.example_es: Es importante saber argumentar tus ideas durante el debate.
cards.example_en: It's important to know how to argue your ideas during the debate.

cards.definition_en: To show or prove something clearly by giving evidence, examples, or a practical display.
cards.example_sentence: The scientist managed to demonstrate his theory with the new experiments.
cards.example_es: El científico logró demostrar su teoría con los nuevos experimentos.
cards.example_en: The scientist managed to demonstrate his theory with the new experiments.

cards.definition_en: to explain or make something clear by using examples, pictures, or diagrams
cards.example_sentence: The teacher used diagrams to illustrate the concept of kinetic energy.
cards.example_es: El profesor utilizó diagramas para ilustrar el concepto de energía cinética.
cards.example_en: The teacher used diagrams to illustrate the concept of kinetic energy.

cards.definition_en: To provide reasons or arguments that show something is right or reasonable.
cards.example_sentence: I need to justify my absence from the meeting with a medical note.
cards.example_es: Necesito justificar mi ausencia en la reunión con una nota médica.
cards.example_en: I need to justify my absence from the meeting with a medical note.

cards.definition_en: To give assistance, encouragement, or approval to someone or something.
cards.example_sentence: My family always supports me in my decisions.
cards.example_es: Mi familia siempre me respalda en mis decisiones.
cards.example_en: My family always supports me in my decisions.

cards.definition_en: To assert the opposite of a statement or claim, thereby denying or opposing it.
cards.example_sentence: You shouldn't contradict your boss in public.
cards.example_es: No deberías contradecir a tu jefe en público.
cards.example_en: You shouldn't contradict your boss in public.

cards.definition_en: To ask about something in order to obtain information or to express doubt about it.
cards.example_sentence: You shouldn't question his motives without knowing the facts.
cards.example_es: No deberías cuestionar sus motivos sin conocer los hechos.
cards.example_en: You shouldn't question his motives without knowing the facts.

cards.definition_en: To express something again using different words, often to clarify or improve it.
cards.example_sentence: Could you rephrase the question to make it clearer?
cards.example_es: ¿Podrías reformular la pregunta para que sea más clara?
cards.example_en: Could you rephrase the question to make it clearer?

cards.definition_en: To assert that something is true, typically without providing proof.
cards.example_sentence: The witness claimed that he saw the suspect at the scene.
cards.example_es: El testigo afirmó que vio al sospechoso en la escena.
cards.example_en: The witness claimed that he saw the suspect at the scene.

cards.definition_en: To propose an idea, plan, or course of action for consideration.
cards.example_sentence: Could you suggest a good restaurant for dinner?
cards.example_es: ¿Podrías sugerir un buen restaurante para cenar?
cards.example_en: Could you suggest a good restaurant for dinner?

cards.definition_en: To point out or show something, making it known.
cards.example_sentence: Please indicate the correct answer on the exam.
cards.example_es: Por favor, señale la respuesta correcta en el examen.
cards.example_en: Please indicate the correct answer on the exam.

cards.definition_en: To emphasize or make something stand out.
cards.example_sentence: I want to highlight the team's achievements in the presentation.
cards.example_es: Quiero destacar los logros del equipo en la presentación.
cards.example_en: I want to highlight the team's achievements in the presentation.

cards.definition_en: To give special importance or attention to something.
cards.example_sentence: The teacher wanted to emphasize the importance of punctuality.
cards.example_es: El profesor quiso enfatizar la importancia de la puntualidad.
cards.example_en: The teacher wanted to emphasize the importance of punctuality.

cards.definition_en: As a result; therefore.
collocations: Consequently, we decided to cancel the event.
cards.example_sentence: Consequently, the project was cancelled.
cards.example_es: En consecuencia, el proyecto fue cancelado.
cards.example_en: Consequently, the project was cancelled.

cards.definition_en: It introduces a contrast or exception to what has just been said.
cards.example_sentence: I wanted to go to the park; however, it started to rain.
cards.example_es: Quería ir al parque, sin embargo empezó a llover.
cards.example_en: I wanted to go to the park; however, it started to rain.

cards.definition_en: In addition; used to introduce an additional point.
cards.example_sentence: Furthermore, the project will be completed before the deadline.
cards.example_es: Además, el proyecto será completado antes de la fecha límite.
cards.example_en: Furthermore, the project will be completed before the deadline.

cards.definition_en: In a contrasting or opposite manner, used to introduce a statement that reverses the previous one.
collocations: Conversely, if you increase the temperature, the pressure drops
Conversely, the results suggest a different trend
cards.example_sentence: He doesn't like coffee; conversely, he prefers tea.
cards.example_es: No le gusta el café; por el contrario, prefiere el té.
cards.example_en: He doesn't like coffee; conversely, he prefers tea.

cards.definition_en: In a similar way; in the same manner.
cards.example_sentence: Similarly, children must respect the rules.
cards.example_es: Del mismo modo, los niños deben respetar las reglas.
cards.example_en: Similarly, children must respect the rules.

cards.definition_en: It means 'in spite of that' or 'nevertheless'.
cards.example_sentence: Nonetheless, he decided to continue with the project.
cards.example_es: No obstante, decidió continuar con el proyecto.
cards.example_en: Nonetheless, he decided to continue with the project.

cards.definition_en: As a result; for that reason.
cards.example_sentence: He studied a lot for the exam; therefore, he got a good grade.
cards.example_es: Estudió mucho para el examen; por lo tanto, obtuvo una buena nota.
cards.example_en: He studied a lot for the exam; therefore, he got a good grade.

cards.definition_en: Used to introduce a statement that differs from or opposes what has just been mentioned.
cards.example_sentence: I prefer tea; she, in contrast, prefers coffee.
cards.example_es: Yo prefiero el té; ella, en cambio, prefiere el café.
cards.example_en: I prefer tea; she, in contrast, prefers coffee.

cards.definition_en: Used to introduce an example.
cards.example_sentence: For instance, if you study every day, you will improve your grades.
cards.example_es: Por ejemplo, si estudias todos los días, mejorarás tus notas.
cards.example_en: For instance, if you study every day, you will improve your grades.

cards.definition_en: A phrase used to introduce a clarification or restatement of something previously said.
collocations: that is to say, the project is delayed
that is to say, in other words
that is to say, we need to act
cards.example_sentence: I didn't like the movie; that is to say, I found it boring.
cards.example_es: No me gustó la película; es decir, la encontré aburrida.
cards.example_en: I didn't like the movie; that is to say, I found it boring.

cards.definition_en: Used to introduce a general summary or overview of a topic.
collocations: Broadly speaking, the results are positive.
Broadly speaking, we can conclude that...
Broadly speaking, it's a good approach.
cards.example_sentence: Broadly speaking, the project involves developing a mobile app for task management.
cards.example_es: A grandes rasgos, el proyecto consiste en desarrollar una aplicación móvil para la gestión de tareas.
cards.example_en: Broadly speaking, the project involves developing a mobile app for task management.

cards.definition_en: Used to introduce the first reason or point in a discussion.
collocations: Why did you do that in the first place?
In the first place, we should consider the costs.
It wasn't necessary in the first place.
cards.example_sentence: In the first place, we must review the documents.
cards.example_es: En primer lugar, debemos revisar los documentos.
cards.example_en: In the first place, we must review the documents.

cards.definition_en: Used to introduce the second item in a list or sequence of points.
cards.example_sentence: Secondly, we must consider the budget.
cards.example_es: En segundo lugar, debemos considerar el presupuesto.
cards.example_en: Secondly, we must consider the budget.

cards.definition_en: A phrase used to indicate that something applies broadly or in a general sense.
collocations: to put it in general terms
in general terms, the result is
in general terms, we can say
cards.example_sentence: In general terms, the project will be completed by the end of the year.
cards.example_es: En términos generales, el proyecto será completado a finales de año.
cards.example_en: In general terms, the project will be completed by the end of the year.

cards.definition_en: In the end, after all other considerations or events have taken place.
cards.example_sentence: Ultimately, we will decide whether the project will go ahead.
cards.example_es: En última instancia, decidiremos si el proyecto sigue adelante.
cards.example_en: Ultimately, we will decide whether the project will go ahead.

cards.definition_en: A phrase used to introduce a statement that the speaker wants to emphasize or draw attention to.
collocations: It should be noted that the data...
It should be noted that this is...
cards.example_sentence: It should be noted that the report will be presented tomorrow.
cards.example_es: Cabe señalar que el informe será presentado mañana.
cards.example_en: It should be noted that the report will be presented tomorrow.

cards.definition_en: A phrase used to introduce a statement that may be debated or supported with reasoning.
collocations: It can be argued that the data supports this
It can be argued that this is the case
It can be argued that there are alternatives
It can be argued that the policy is ineffective
cards.example_sentence: It can be argued that the economy will grow next year.
cards.example_es: Se puede sostener que la economía crecerá el próximo año.
cards.example_en: It can be argued that the economy will grow next year.

cards.definition_en: A phrase used to indicate that something appears clear or obvious.
collocations: It seems evident that the data show...
It seems evident that we need to act
It seems evident that this is the case
cards.example_sentence: It seems evident that the evidence supports his theory.
cards.example_es: Parece evidente que la evidencia apoya su teoría.
cards.example_en: It seems evident that the evidence supports his theory.

cards.definition_en: A statement indicating that something is generally approved or recognized by many people.
cards.example_sentence: It is widely accepted that recycling helps the environment.
cards.example_es: Está ampliamente aceptado que el reciclaje ayuda al medio ambiente.
cards.example_en: It is widely accepted that recycling helps the environment.

cards.definition_en: To stay in a state of uncertainty.
cards.example_sentence: The outcome of the trial will remain uncertain.
cards.example_es: El resultado del juicio seguirá permaneciendo incierto.
cards.example_en: The outcome of the trial will remain uncertain.

cards.definition_en: To consider something carefully when making a decision.
cards.example_sentence: You should take into consideration the doctor's recommendations before starting the treatment.
cards.example_es: Debes tener en cuenta las recomendaciones del médico antes de iniciar el tratamiento.
cards.example_en: You should take into consideration the doctor's recommendations before starting the treatment.

cards.definition_en: to form a judgment or inference based on evidence
collocations: draw a conclusion from the data
draw a conclusion based on the evidence
cards.example_sentence: After reviewing the data, we can draw a clear conclusion.
cards.example_es: Después de revisar los datos, podemos sacar una conclusión clara.
cards.example_en: After reviewing the data, we can draw a clear conclusion.

cards.definition_en: to arrive at a decision or judgment after considering the evidence
cards.example_sentence: After analyzing the data, we reached a conclusion.
cards.example_es: Después de analizar los datos, llegamos a una conclusión.
cards.example_en: After analyzing the data, we reached a conclusion.

cards.definition_en: A phrase used to introduce a final statement or summary.
collocations: By way of conclusion, we can see that...
By way of conclusion, the study suggests...
By way of conclusion, it is evident that...
By way of conclusion, I recommend...
cards.example_sentence: By way of conclusion, we can say that the project was a success.
cards.example_es: A modo de conclusión, podemos decir que el proyecto fue un éxito.
cards.example_en: By way of conclusion, we can say that the project was a success.

cards.definition_en: A written document presenting the author's research and findings, typically submitted for an academic degree.
cards.example_sentence: I am working on my doctoral thesis.
cards.example_es: Estoy trabajando en mi tesis de doctorado.
cards.example_en: I am working on my doctoral thesis.

cards.definition_en: A statement or set of reasons presented in opposition to another argument.
cards.example_sentence: His statement was a counterargument to the proposed theory.
cards.example_es: Su argumento era la antítesis de la teoría propuesta.
cards.example_en: His statement was a counterargument to the proposed theory.

cards.definition_en: a person's attitude or position on a particular issue
cards.example_sentence: His stance on the change was very clear.
cards.example_es: Su postura frente al cambio fue muy clara.
cards.example_en: His stance on the change was very clear.

cards.definition_en: The mental process of drawing conclusions or making inferences based on evidence or logical principles.
cards.example_sentence: His reasoning was impeccable during the debate.
cards.example_es: Su razonamiento fue impecable durante el debate.
cards.example_en: His reasoning was impeccable during the debate.

cards.definition_en: A statement or proposition that serves as a basis for reasoning or argument.
cards.example_sentence: The premise of the argument is that all human beings seek happiness.
cards.example_es: La premisa del argumento es que todos los seres humanos buscan la felicidad.
cards.example_en: The premise of the argument is that all human beings seek happiness.

cards.definition_en: A conclusion drawn from evidence and reasoning.
cards.example_sentence: Based on the data, he made an inference about the future behavior of the market.
cards.example_es: A partir de los datos, hizo una inferencia sobre el comportamiento futuro del mercado.
cards.example_en: Based on the data, he made an inference about the future behavior of the market.

cards.definition_en: The quality of being logically sound or legally acceptable.
cards.example_sentence: The validity of the contract expired on December 31st.
cards.example_es: La validez del contrato expiró el 31 de diciembre.
cards.example_en: The validity of the contract expired on December 31st.

cards.definition_en: The quality of being trustworthy or dependable.
cards.example_sentence: The reliability of this car is impressive.
cards.example_es: La fiabilidad de este coche es impresionante.
cards.example_en: The reliability of this car is impressive.

cards.definition_en: A tendency to favor or prejudice a particular perspective, group, or outcome.
cards.example_sentence: The bias of the survey made the results unrepresentative.
cards.example_es: El sesgo de la encuesta hizo que los resultados no fueran representativos.
cards.example_en: The bias of the survey made the results unrepresentative.

cards.definition_en: The quality of being objective, or the ability to consider facts without personal bias or emotion.
cards.example_sentence: The scientist must maintain objectivity when analyzing the data.
cards.example_es: El científico debe mantener la objetividad al analizar los datos.
cards.example_en: The scientist must maintain objectivity when analyzing the data.

cards.definition_en: The quality of being based on personal feelings, tastes, or opinions rather than objective facts.
cards.example_sentence: The subjectivity of the author is reflected in every chapter of the book.
cards.example_es: La subjetividad del autor se refleja en cada capítulo del libro.
cards.example_en: The subjectivity of the author is reflected in every chapter of the book.

cards.definition_en: A statistical measure that describes the extent to which two variables change together.
cards.example_sentence: There is a strong correlation between sugar consumption and the incidence of diabetes.
cards.example_es: Existe una fuerte correlación entre el consumo de azúcar y la incidencia de diabetes.
cards.example_en: There is a strong correlation between sugar consumption and the incidence of diabetes.

cards.definition_en: The relationship in which one event or action causes another to occur.
cards.example_sentence: The causation between smoking and lung cancer is well established.
cards.example_es: La causalidad entre fumar y el cáncer de pulmón está bien establecida.
cards.example_en: The causation between smoking and lung cancer is well established.

cards.definition_en: The act of giving out or spreading something, or the way in which something is shared or dispersed.
cards.example_sentence: The distribution of the resources was carried out equitably.
cards.example_es: La distribución de los recursos se realizó de manera equitativa.
cards.example_en: The distribution of the resources was carried out equitably.

cards.definition_en: A departure from a standard, norm, or expected course.
cards.example_sentence: The deviation from the road was minimal.
cards.example_es: La desviación del camino fue mínima.
cards.example_en: The deviation from the road was minimal.

cards.definition_en: The quality of being consistent, or the ability to remain the same over time or across situations.
cards.example_sentence: The consistency of the yogurt is perfect for breakfast.
cards.example_es: La consistencia del yogur es perfecta para el desayuno.
cards.example_en: The consistency of the yogurt is perfect for breakfast.

cards.definition_en: The quality or state of being correct, exact, or precise.
cards.example_sentence: The accuracy of the Swiss watch is impressive.
cards.example_es: La precisión del reloj suizo es impresionante.
cards.example_en: The accuracy of the Swiss watch is impressive.

cards.definition_en: The quality of being relevant or applicable to a particular matter.
cards.example_sentence: The relevance of scientific data is essential for making informed decisions.
cards.example_es: La relevancia de los datos científicos es fundamental para tomar decisiones informadas.
cards.example_en: The relevance of scientific data is essential for making informed decisions.

cards.definition_en: The quality of being suitable or fitting for a particular purpose, situation, or person.
cards.example_sentence: The appropriateness of his remarks improved the group's discussion.
cards.example_es: La pertinencia de sus comentarios mejoró la discusión del grupo.
cards.example_en: The appropriateness of his remarks improved the group's discussion.

cards.definition_en: A subset of a population that accurately reflects the characteristics of the whole group.
collocations: a representative sample of the population
cards.example_sentence: For the study, we took a representative sample of the population.
cards.example_es: Para el estudio, tomamos una muestra representativa de la población.
cards.example_en: For the study, we took a representative sample of the population.

cards.definition_en: Selection criteria are the standards or requirements used to choose among alternatives.
cards.example_sentence: The selection criteria for the scholarship program is academic performance.
cards.example_es: El criterio de selección para el programa de becas es el rendimiento académico.
cards.example_en: The selection criteria for the scholarship program is academic performance.

cards.definition_en: To gather or obtain information, typically for analysis or record-keeping.
cards.example_sentence: The team needs to collect data before presenting the report.
cards.example_es: El equipo necesita recopilar datos antes de presentar el informe.
cards.example_en: The team needs to collect data before presenting the report.

cards.definition_en: To manipulate or handle data in order to extract information or perform operations on it.
cards.example_sentence: We need to process data before generating the report.
cards.example_es: Necesitamos procesar datos antes de generar el informe.
cards.example_en: We need to process data before generating the report.

cards.definition_en: Based on or derived from evidence.
cards.example_sentence: The new treatment is evidence-based, relying on scientific data.
cards.example_es: El nuevo tratamiento está respaldado por evidencia científica.
cards.example_en: The new treatment is evidence-based, relying on scientific data.

cards.definition_en: Considerable in amount, size, or importance; significant.
cards.example_sentence: The increase in investment was substantial this year.
cards.example_es: El aumento de la inversión fue sustancial este año.
cards.example_en: The increase in investment was substantial this year.

cards.definition_en: Existing or situated at the edge or margin, or being only barely sufficient.
cards.example_sentence: The marginal increase in price will not affect most consumers.
cards.example_es: El aumento marginal del precio no afectará a la mayoría de los consumidores.
cards.example_en: The marginal increase in price will not affect most consumers.

cards.definition_en: Important or notable enough to be worthy of attention.
cards.example_sentence: This finding is significant for the research.
cards.example_es: Este hallazgo es significativo para la investigación.
cards.example_en: This finding is significant for the research.

cards.definition_en: so small or unimportant that it is not worth considering
cards.example_sentence: The impact of that mistake is negligible on the final result.
cards.example_es: El impacto de ese error es insignificante en el resultado final.
cards.example_en: The impact of that mistake is negligible on the final result.

cards.definition_en: strictness or exactness in standards, procedures, or analysis
cards.example_sentence: The rigor of the scientific method ensures reliable results.
cards.example_es: La rigurosidad del método científico garantiza resultados confiables.
cards.example_en: The rigor of the scientific method ensures reliable results.

cards.definition_en: The quality of being logical, consistent, and forming a unified whole.
cards.example_sentence: The coherence of the argument made everyone accept it.
cards.example_es: La coherencia del argumento hizo que todos lo aceptaran.
cards.example_en: The coherence of the argument made everyone accept it.

cards.definition_en: The quality of being clear, easily understood, or transparent.
cards.example_sentence: The clarity of the lake's water was impressive.
cards.example_es: La claridad del agua del lago era impresionante.
cards.example_en: The clarity of the lake's water was impressive.

cards.definition_en: The quality of being brief and to the point in speech or writing.
cards.example_sentence: Conciseness is essential when drafting brief reports.
cards.example_es: La concisión es esencial al redactar informes breves.
cards.example_en: Conciseness is essential when drafting brief reports.

cards.definition_en: To give more detail or expand upon something.
cards.example_sentence: Could you elaborate on your explanation of the process?
cards.example_es: ¿Podrías ampliar tu explicación sobre el proceso?
cards.example_en: Could you elaborate on your explanation of the process?

cards.definition_en: To give a brief statement of the main points of something.
cards.example_sentence: Please summarize the article in three sentences.
cards.example_es: Por favor, resume el artículo en tres frases.
cards.example_en: Please summarize the article in three sentences.

cards.definition_en: A restatement of a text or passage using different words, especially to achieve greater clarity.
cards.example_sentence: Can you paraphrase what I said to make it clearer?
cards.example_es: ¿Puedes parafrasear lo que dije para que sea más claro?
cards.example_en: Can you paraphrase what I said to make it clearer?

cards.definition_en: To quote or refer to a source as evidence or support.
cards.example_sentence: The article cites several experts on climate change.
cards.example_es: El artículo cita a varios expertos sobre el cambio climático.
cards.example_en: The article cites several experts on climate change.

cards.definition_en: A source of information that is cited or consulted.
cards.example_sentence: It is important to reference all sources in your research paper.
cards.example_es: Es importante referenciar todas las fuentes en tu trabajo de investigación.
cards.example_en: It is important to reference all sources in your research paper.

cards.definition_en: Notes placed at the bottom of a page that give additional information or citations.
cards.example_sentence: The footnotes explain the technical terms of the article.
cards.example_es: Las notas a pie de página explican los términos técnicos del artículo.
cards.example_en: The footnotes explain the technical terms of the article.

cards.definition_en: A supplementary section at the end of a book or document, or a small organ attached to the large intestine.
cards.example_sentence: The appendix of the book contains additional information about the history.
cards.example_es: El apéndice del libro contiene información adicional sobre la historia.
cards.example_en: The appendix of the book contains additional information about the history.

cards.definition_en: A brief overview of a document's main points, usually placed at the beginning.
cards.example_sentence: The executive summary of the report highlights the main findings.
cards.example_es: El resumen ejecutivo del informe destaca los principales hallazgos.
cards.example_en: The executive summary of the report highlights the main findings.

cards.definition_en: A question that guides the focus and direction of a research study.
cards.example_sentence: The research question of my thesis is how climate change affects biodiversity.
cards.example_es: La pregunta de investigación de mi tesis es cómo afecta el cambio climático a la biodiversidad.
cards.example_en: The research question of my thesis is how climate change affects biodiversity.

cards.definition_en: The range or extent of topics covered by the study.
collocations: the scope of the study includes
within the scope of the study
the scope of the study was limited
cards.example_sentence: The scope of the study is molecular biology.
cards.example_es: El ámbito de estudio de la investigación es la biología molecular.
cards.example_en: The scope of the study is molecular biology.

cards.definition_en: A specific area or focus within a broader field of study or investigation.
cards.example_sentence: My research line focuses on artificial intelligence.
cards.example_es: Mi línea de investigación se centra en la inteligencia artificial.
cards.example_en: My research line focuses on artificial intelligence.

cards.definition_en: A set of ideas and concepts that provides a structure for understanding or analyzing something.
cards.example_sentence: The researcher presented a new conceptual framework to understand urban migration.
cards.example_es: El investigador presentó un nuevo marco conceptual para entender la migración urbana.
cards.example_en: The researcher presented a new conceptual framework to understand urban migration.

cards.definition_en: A research gap is an area or topic that has not yet been studied or is insufficiently explored in existing literature.
cards.example_sentence: The study identifies a research gap in the relationship between diet and mental health.
cards.example_es: El estudio identifica un vacío de investigación en la relación entre la dieta y la salud mental.
cards.example_en: The study identifies a research gap in the relationship between diet and mental health.

cards.definition_en: A significant or important result discovered in a study or investigation.
cards.example_sentence: The key finding of the study was the relationship between diet and cardiovascular health.
cards.example_es: El hallazgo clave del estudio fue la relación entre la dieta y la salud cardiovascular.
cards.example_en: The key finding of the study was the relationship between diet and cardiovascular health.

cards.definition_en: A highly influential piece of research or writing that forms the basis for later developments.
cards.example_sentence: This article is a seminal work in the field of evolutionary biology.
cards.example_es: Este artículo es un trabajo seminal en el campo de la biología evolutiva.
cards.example_en: This article is a seminal work in the field of evolutionary biology.

cards.definition_en: A contribution that is new, unique, or created for the first time.
cards.example_sentence: His original contribution to the project was essential.
cards.example_es: Su contribución original al proyecto fue fundamental.
cards.example_en: His original contribution to the project was essential.

cards.definition_en: Additional possible paths or options for exploration.
cards.example_sentence: We will explore further avenues to improve the project.
cards.example_es: Exploraremos nuevas líneas futuras para mejorar el proyecto.
cards.example_en: We will explore further avenues to improve the project.

title: News and Society
description: Advanced vocabulary for current affairs, social issues, media, technology, and global events.

cards.definition_en: A headline is a brief, attention‑grabbing title that appears at the top of a news article or other written piece.
cards.example_sentence: The headline in the newspaper announced the team's victory.
cards.example_es: El titular del periódico anunciaba la victoria del equipo.
cards.example_en: The headline in the newspaper announced the team's victory.

cards.definition_en: The reporting of an event or topic by various media outlets.
cards.example_sentence: The media coverage of the accident was extensive and continuous.
cards.example_es: La cobertura mediática del accidente fue extensa y constante.
cards.example_en: The media coverage of the accident was extensive and continuous.

cards.definition_en: A feature article is a detailed, in‑depth newspaper or magazine story that explores a topic more comprehensively than a news report.
cards.example_sentence: I read a feature article about the climate crisis in the newspaper.
cards.example_es: Leí un artículo de fondo sobre la crisis climática en el periódico.
