cards.example_en: I read a feature article about the climate crisis in the newspaper.

cards.definition_en: An article expressing the opinion of the editor or the publication.
cards.example_sentence: I read the editorial in the newspaper about the climate crisis.
cards.example_es: Leí la editorial del periódico sobre la crisis climática.
cards.example_en: I read the editorial in the newspaper about the climate crisis.

cards.definition_en: A source that can be trusted to provide accurate and trustworthy information.
cards.example_sentence: This article is a reliable source for studying history.
cards.example_es: Este artículo es una fuente fiable para estudiar la historia.
cards.example_en: This article is a reliable source for studying history.

cards.definition_en: False or inaccurate information that is spread deliberately or unintentionally.
cards.example_sentence: Misinformation about the vaccine is causing fear.
cards.example_es: La desinformación sobre la vacuna está generando miedo.
cards.example_en: Misinformation about the vaccine is causing fear.

cards.definition_en: Information that is incorrect or misleading.
cards.example_sentence: Don't believe everything you read online; much of that information is false.
cards.example_es: No creas todo lo que lees en internet; mucha de esa información es falsa.
cards.example_en: Don't believe everything you read online; much of that information is false.

cards.definition_en: Urgent or newly received news that is reported immediately.
cards.example_sentence: Breaking news! The president has announced his resignation.
cards.example_es: ¡Noticia de última hora! El presidente ha anunciado su renuncia.
cards.example_en: Breaking news! The president has announced his resignation.

cards.definition_en: Live coverage is the real-time reporting or broadcasting of an event as it happens.
cards.example_sentence: The live coverage of the concert will be streamed on our website.
cards.example_es: La cobertura en directo del concierto se transmitirá en nuestro sitio web.
cards.example_en: The live coverage of the concert will be streamed on our website.

cards.definition_en: A change that adds new information or improves something to make it current.
cards.example_sentence: The update of the software improved the device's performance.
cards.example_es: La actualización del software mejoró el rendimiento del dispositivo.
cards.example_en: The update of the software improved the device's performance.

cards.definition_en: A survey that asks people for their views on a particular topic.
cards.example_sentence: The opinion poll showed that the majority supports the measure.
cards.example_es: La encuesta de opinión mostró que la mayoría apoya la medida.
cards.example_en: The opinion poll showed that the majority supports the measure.

cards.definition_en: The percentage or number of eligible voters who actually cast a ballot in an election.
cards.example_sentence: Voter turnout in the last election was 70%.
cards.example_es: La participación electoral en las últimas elecciones fue del 70%.
cards.example_en: Voter turnout in the last election was 70%.

cards.definition_en: A coordinated effort by a candidate or political party to win votes in an upcoming election.
cards.example_sentence: The election campaign began last week and the debates are already being heard.
cards.example_es: La campaña electoral comenzó la semana pasada y ya se están escuchando los debates.
cards.example_en: The election campaign began last week and the debates are already being heard.

cards.definition_en: A public debate is an open discussion where participants exchange differing views on a topic in front of an audience.
cards.example_sentence: The public debate on climate change attracted thousands of viewers.
cards.example_es: El debate público sobre el cambio climático atrajo a miles de espectadores.
cards.example_en: The public debate on climate change attracted thousands of viewers.

cards.definition_en: The process of dividing something into opposing groups or of aligning electric charges in opposite directions.
cards.example_sentence: Political polarization in the country has increased in recent years.
cards.example_es: La polarización política en el país ha aumentado en los últimos años.
cards.example_en: Political polarization in the country has increased in recent years.

cards.definition_en: A political approach that seeks to appeal to ordinary people who feel their concerns are ignored by established elites.
cards.example_sentence: Populism has gained ground in several Latin American countries.
cards.example_es: El populismo ha ganado terreno en varios países de América Latina.
cards.example_en: Populism has gained ground in several Latin American countries.

cards.definition_en: The act or system of governing, or the way an organization or society is managed and controlled.
cards.example_sentence: The governance of the company is based on transparency and employee participation.
cards.example_es: La gobernanza de la empresa se basa en la transparencia y la participación de los empleados.
cards.example_en: The governance of the company is based on transparency and employee participation.

cards.definition_en: The quality of being clear, open, or easily understood, or the condition of allowing light to pass through so that objects behind can be seen.
cards.example_sentence: Government transparency is essential for citizens' trust.
cards.example_es: La transparencia del gobierno es esencial para la confianza de los ciudadanos.
cards.example_en: Government transparency is essential for citizens' trust.

cards.definition_en: The obligation to accept responsibility for one's actions and decisions.
cards.example_sentence: Accountability is essential for transparency in government.
cards.example_es: La rendición de cuentas es fundamental para la transparencia en el gobierno.
cards.example_en: Accountability is essential for transparency in government.

cards.definition_en: Dishonest or fraudulent conduct by those in power, often involving bribery or the misuse of authority for personal gain.
cards.example_sentence: Corruption in the government has decreased thanks to the new laws.
cards.example_es: La corrupción en el gobierno ha disminuido gracias a las nuevas leyes.
cards.example_en: Corruption in the government has decreased thanks to the new laws.

cards.definition_en: A condition of being unequal, especially in status, rights, or opportunities.
cards.example_sentence: Salary inequality between men and women remains a serious problem.
cards.example_es: La desigualdad salarial entre hombres y mujeres sigue siendo un problema grave.
cards.example_en: Salary inequality between men and women remains a serious problem.

cards.definition_en: The movement of individuals or groups within or between social classes in a society.
cards.example_sentence: Social mobility is a key indicator of equal opportunity in a society.
cards.example_es: La movilidad social es un indicador clave de la igualdad de oportunidades en una sociedad.
cards.example_en: Social mobility is a key indicator of equal opportunity in a society.

cards.definition_en: The amount of money required to cover basic expenses such as housing, food, taxes, and healthcare in a specific location.
cards.example_sentence: The cost of living in Madrid is very high.
cards.example_es: El costo de vida en Madrid es muy alto.
cards.example_en: The cost of living in Madrid is very high.

cards.definition_en: A general increase in prices and a corresponding decline in the purchasing power of money.
cards.example_sentence: Inflation has increased the cost of food.
cards.example_es: La inflación ha aumentado el costo de los alimentos.
cards.example_en: Inflation has increased the cost of food.

cards.definition_en: A period of economic decline marked by reduced trade, production, and rising unemployment.
cards.example_sentence: The recession has caused an increase in unemployment in the country.
cards.example_es: La recesión ha provocado un aumento del desempleo en el país.
cards.example_en: The recession has caused an increase in unemployment in the country.

cards.definition_en: A period when an economy regains growth and improves after a downturn.
cards.example_sentence: The country's economic recovery has been faster than expected.
cards.example_es: La recuperación económica del país ha sido más rápida de lo esperado.
cards.example_en: The country's economic recovery has been faster than expected.

cards.definition_en: The supply of workers and the demand for jobs in an economy, encompassing employment conditions and opportunities.
cards.example_sentence: The labor market is experiencing a high demand for technology professionals.
cards.example_es: El mercado laboral está experimentando una alta demanda de profesionales de tecnología.
cards.example_en: The labor market is experiencing a high demand for technology professionals.

cards.definition_en: A situation in which the supply of affordable housing is insufficient to meet demand, causing widespread difficulty in obtaining suitable homes.
cards.example_sentence: The housing crisis is forcing many families to seek shelter in temporary homes.
cards.example_es: La crisis habitacional está obligando a muchas familias a buscar refugio en viviendas temporales.
cards.example_en: The housing crisis is forcing many families to seek shelter in temporary homes.

cards.definition_en: A person who lacks a permanent residence or home.
cards.example_sentence: After the fire, he was left homeless and had to find shelter.
cards.example_es: Después del incendio, quedó sin techo y tuvo que buscar refugio.
cards.example_en: After the fire, he was left homeless and had to find shelter.

cards.definition_en: The organized provision of medical services to maintain or improve people's health.
cards.example_sentence: Access to healthcare is a fundamental right.
cards.example_es: El acceso a la atención sanitaria es un derecho fundamental.
cards.example_en: Access to healthcare is a fundamental right.

cards.definition_en: A state of psychological well‑being that enables a person to handle daily stresses, work productively, and contribute to society.
cards.example_sentence: Mental health is as important as physical health.
cards.example_es: La salud mental es tan importante como la salud física.
cards.example_en: Mental health is as important as physical health.

cards.definition_en: A state of being comfortable, healthy, or happy.
cards.example_sentence: The exercise program improves the participants' well-being.
cards.example_es: El programa de ejercicios mejora el bienestar de los participantes.
cards.example_en: The exercise program improves the participants' well-being.

cards.definition_en: A demographic situation in which a large proportion of the population is elderly.
cards.example_sentence: The aging population is a challenge for health systems.
cards.example_es: El envejecimiento de la población es un desafío para los sistemas de salud.
cards.example_en: The aging population is a challenge for health systems.

cards.definition_en: The movement of people or groups from one location to another, typically for settlement, work, or environmental reasons.
cards.example_sentence: The migration of birds to the south begins in autumn.
cards.example_es: La migración de aves hacia el sur comienza en otoño.
cards.example_en: The migration of birds to the south begins in autumn.

cards.definition_en: A person who has been forced to leave their country to escape war, persecution, or disaster.
cards.example_sentence: The refugee found a new home in the city.
cards.example_es: El refugiado encontró un nuevo hogar en la ciudad.
cards.example_en: The refugee found a new home in the city.

cards.definition_en: The process of combining separate parts into a unified whole.
cards.example_sentence: The integration of the new employees into the company was very quick.
cards.example_es: La integración de los nuevos empleados en la empresa fue muy rápida.
cards.example_en: The integration of the new employees into the company was very quick.

cards.definition_en: Unfair or prejudicial treatment of individuals or groups based on characteristics such as race, gender, age, or other attributes.
cards.example_sentence: Discrimination in the workplace is unacceptable.
cards.example_es: La discriminación en el lugar de trabajo es inaceptable.
cards.example_en: Discrimination in the workplace is unacceptable.

cards.definition_en: The act or state of including someone or something, or the condition of being included.
cards.example_sentence: The inclusion of students with special needs in the classroom benefits all students.
cards.example_es: La inclusión de estudiantes con necesidades especiales en el aula beneficia a todos los alumnos.
cards.example_en: The inclusion of students with special needs in the classroom benefits all students.

cards.definition_en: The quality of being easy to reach, use, understand, or obtain, especially for people with disabilities.
cards.example_sentence: The accessibility of the website is essential for users with disabilities.
cards.example_es: La accesibilidad del sitio web es fundamental para los usuarios con discapacidades.
cards.example_en: The accessibility of the website is essential for users with disabilities.

cards.definition_en: The gap between individuals, households, or regions in access to and use of information and communication technologies.
cards.example_sentence: The digital divide affects rural communities.
cards.example_es: La brecha digital afecta a las comunidades rurales.
cards.example_en: The digital divide affects rural communities.

cards.definition_en: The protection of personal information from unauthorized access or disclosure.
cards.example_sentence: Data privacy is essential to protect personal information.
cards.example_es: La privacidad de datos es fundamental para proteger la información personal.
cards.example_en: Data privacy is essential to protect personal information.

cards.definition_en: The practice of protecting computers, networks, and data from unauthorized access, attacks, or damage.
cards.example_sentence: Cybersecurity is essential to protect the company's data.
cards.example_es: La ciberseguridad es esencial para proteger los datos de la empresa.
cards.example_en: Cybersecurity is essential to protect the company's data.

cards.definition_en: The close monitoring of people, places, or activities, especially for security or intelligence purposes.
cards.example_sentence: Surveillance of the park was intensified after the robbery.
cards.example_es: La vigilancia del parque se intensificó después del robo.
cards.example_en: Surveillance of the park was intensified after the robbery.

cards.definition_en: A step-by-step procedure or set of rules for solving a problem or performing a computation.
cards.example_sentence: The algorithm found the information quickly.
cards.example_es: El algoritmo de búsqueda encontró la información rápidamente.
cards.example_en: The algorithm found the information quickly.

cards.definition_en: Artificial intelligence is the branch of computer science that develops systems capable of performing tasks that normally require human intelligence.
cards.example_sentence: Artificial intelligence is transforming the medical industry.
cards.example_es: La inteligencia artificial está transformando la industria médica.
cards.example_en: Artificial intelligence is transforming the medical industry.

cards.definition_en: The use of technology or machines to perform tasks without human intervention.
cards.example_sentence: Automation of manufacturing processes has increased efficiency.
cards.example_es: La automatización de los procesos de fabricación ha aumentado la eficiencia.
cards.example_en: Automation of manufacturing processes has increased efficiency.

cards.definition_en: False or misleading information that is generated, spread, or amplified by algorithms.
cards.example_sentence: Algorithmic misinformation spreads quickly on social media.
cards.example_es: La desinformación algorítmica se propaga rápidamente en las redes sociales.
cards.example_en: Algorithmic misinformation spreads quickly on social media.

cards.definition_en: A long-term alteration in Earth's average weather patterns, primarily caused by human activities that increase greenhouse gases.
cards.example_sentence: Climate change is affecting the glaciers.
cards.example_es: El cambio climático está afectando a los glaciares.
cards.example_en: Climate change is affecting the glaciers.

cards.definition_en: A long-term rise in Earth's average atmospheric temperature caused primarily by human activities that increase greenhouse gas concentrations.
collocations: global warming is a serious issue
cards.example_sentence: Global warming is causing extreme weather events.
cards.example_es: El calentamiento global está provocando fenómenos climáticos extremos.
cards.example_en: Global warming is causing extreme weather events.

cards.definition_en: Substances, especially gases or particles, released into the atmosphere, typically as a result of industrial processes or combustion.
cards.example_sentence: The CO₂ emissions from the factory have increased this year.
cards.example_es: Las emisiones de CO₂ de la fábrica han aumentado este año.
cards.example_en: The CO₂ emissions from the factory have increased this year.

cards.definition_en: The total amount of greenhouse gases emitted directly or indirectly by a person, organization, event, or product.
cards.example_sentence: Reducing our carbon footprint is essential to combat climate change.
cards.example_es: Reducir nuestra huella de carbono es esencial para combatir el cambio climático.
cards.example_en: Reducing our carbon footprint is essential to combat climate change.

cards.definition_en: Energy obtained from naturally replenished sources such as sunlight, wind, rain, tides, waves, and geothermal heat.
cards.example_sentence: Renewable energy reduces dependence on fossil fuels.
cards.example_es: La energía renovable reduce la dependencia de los combustibles fósiles.
cards.example_en: Renewable energy reduces dependence on fossil fuels.

cards.definition_en: Natural resources such as coal, oil, and natural gas formed from ancient organic matter and used as energy sources.
cards.example_sentence: Fossil fuels are the main source of energy in the world.
cards.example_es: Los combustibles fósiles son la principal fuente de energía en el mundo.
cards.example_en: Fossil fuels are the main source of energy in the world.

cards.definition_en: A prolonged period of abnormally low rainfall that results in a shortage of water.
cards.example_sentence: The drought has reduced the corn harvest this year.
cards.example_es: La sequía ha reducido la cosecha de maíz este año.
cards.example_en: The drought has reduced the corn harvest this year.

cards.definition_en: A large amount of water that covers land which is normally dry.
cards.example_sentence: The flood destroyed several houses in the town.
cards.example_es: La inundación destruyó varias casas en el pueblo.
cards.example_en: The flood destroyed several houses in the town.

cards.definition_en: A prolonged period of excessively hot weather.
cards.example_sentence: This week there was a heatwave that reached 40 degrees.
cards.example_es: Esta semana hubo una ola de calor que alcanzó los 40 grados.
cards.example_en: This week there was a heatwave that reached 40 degrees.

cards.definition_en: The protection, preservation, or careful management of natural resources and the environment.
cards.example_sentence: The conservation of forests is essential to combat climate change.
cards.example_es: La conservación de los bosques es esencial para combatir el cambio climático.
cards.example_en: The conservation of forests is essential to combat climate change.

cards.definition_en: The variety of living organisms and their ecological interactions within a given area or across the planet.
cards.example_sentence: Biodiversity is essential for the balance of the ecosystem.
cards.example_es: La biodiversidad es esencial para el equilibrio del ecosistema.
cards.example_en: Biodiversity is essential for the balance of the ecosystem.

cards.definition_en: The removal or clearing of trees and forested land, typically for human use such as agriculture or development.
cards.example_sentence: Deforestation is causing habitat loss in the Amazon.
cards.example_es: La deforestación está provocando la pérdida de hábitats en la Amazonía.
cards.example_en: Deforestation is causing habitat loss in the Amazon.

cards.definition_en: Pollution is the introduction of harmful substances or energy into the environment.
cards.example_sentence: Air pollution is worsening the health of the population.
cards.example_es: La contaminación del aire está empeorando la salud de la población.
cards.example_en: Air pollution is worsening the health of the population.

cards.definition_en: Waste is material, resources, or energy that is discarded, unused, or considered useless.
cards.example_sentence: Food waste is a serious problem.
cards.example_es: El desperdicio de alimentos es un problema grave.
cards.example_en: Food waste is a serious problem.

cards.definition_en: The process of collecting and converting waste materials into new, usable products.
cards.example_sentence: Recycling helps protect the environment.
cards.example_es: El reciclaje ayuda a proteger el medio ambiente.
cards.example_en: Recycling helps protect the environment.

cards.definition_en: An economic system that aims to minimize waste and maximize resource use by reusing, repairing, refurbishing, and recycling materials and products.
cards.example_sentence: The company has adopted the circular economy to reduce its waste.
cards.example_es: La empresa ha adoptado la economía circular para reducir sus residuos.
cards.example_en: The company has adopted the circular economy to reduce its waste.

cards.definition_en: Food security is the condition in which all people have reliable access to sufficient, safe, and nutritious food.
cards.example_sentence: Food security is essential for sustainable development.
cards.example_es: La seguridad alimentaria es esencial para el desarrollo sostenible.
cards.example_en: Food security is essential for sustainable development.

cards.definition_en: A condition in which there is insufficient available water to meet the needs of a region or population.
cards.example_sentence: Water scarcity is affecting millions of people worldwide.
cards.example_es: La escasez de agua está afectando a millones de personas en todo el mundo.
cards.example_en: Water scarcity is affecting millions of people worldwide.

cards.definition_en: A network of organizations, activities, and resources that moves a product or service from supplier to customer.
cards.example_sentence: The company is optimizing its supply chain to reduce costs.
cards.example_es: La empresa está optimizando su cadena de suministro para reducir costos.
cards.example_en: The company is optimizing its supply chain to reduce costs.

cards.definition_en: A disturbance or interruption that prevents something from continuing smoothly.
cards.example_sentence: The disruption of the power supply left the city without electricity.
cards.example_es: La interrupción del suministro eléctrico dejó a la ciudad sin luz.
cards.example_en: The disruption of the power supply left the city without electricity.

cards.definition_en: A rapid increase or intensification, especially of a conflict, problem, or situation.
cards.example_sentence: The escalation of the conflict surprised everyone.
cards.example_es: La escalada del conflicto sorprendió a todos.
cards.example_en: The escalation of the conflict surprised everyone.

cards.definition_en: A temporary suspension of fighting between opposing sides.
cards.example_sentence: Ceasefire, before more people get hurt.
cards.example_es: Alto el fuego, antes de que más personas resulten heridas.
cards.example_en: Ceasefire, before more people get hurt.

cards.definition_en: A penalty imposed for violating a law, rule, or regulation.
cards.example_sentence: The committee decided to impose a sanction of ten thousand euros on the club for violating the rules.
cards.example_es: El comité decidió imponer una sanción de diez mil euros al club por incumplir las normas.
cards.example_en: The committee decided to impose a sanction of ten thousand euros on the club for violating the rules.

cards.definition_en: Assistance given to people in need, especially during crises or disasters, to alleviate suffering and protect life and health.
cards.example_sentence: Humanitarian aid arrived in the area affected by the earthquake.
cards.example_es: La ayuda humanitaria llegó a la zona afectada por el terremoto.
cards.example_en: Humanitarian aid arrived in the area affected by the earthquake.

cards.definition_en: The involuntary movement of people from their homes or habitual residence, usually caused by conflict, persecution, or natural disasters.
cards.example_sentence: The forced displacement of thousands of families has created a humanitarian crisis.
cards.example_es: El desplazamiento forzado de miles de familias ha creado una crisis humanitaria.
cards.example_en: The forced displacement of thousands of families has created a humanitarian crisis.

cards.definition_en: Fundamental rights and freedoms that belong to every person simply because they are human.
cards.example_sentence: Human rights must be respected worldwide.
cards.example_es: Los derechos humanos deben ser respetados en todo el mundo.
cards.example_en: Human rights must be respected worldwide.

cards.definition_en: The right to express one's opinions publicly without censorship or restraint.
cards.example_sentence: Freedom of speech is a fundamental right in a democracy.
cards.example_es: La libertad de expresión es un derecho fundamental en una democracia.
cards.example_en: Freedom of speech is a fundamental right in a democracy.

cards.definition_en: The principle that all individuals and institutions, including the government, are bound by and accountable to publicly known laws.
cards.example_sentence: In a rule of law system, all citizens are subject to the same legislation.
cards.example_es: En un Estado de derecho, todos los ciudadanos están sujetos a la misma legislación.
cards.example_en: In a rule of law system, all citizens are subject to the same legislation.

cards.definition_en: A change made to improve a system, institution, or practice.
cards.example_sentence: The reform of the education system will be approved next month.
cards.example_es: La reforma del sistema educativo será aprobada el próximo mes.
cards.example_en: The reform of the education system will be approved next month.

cards.definition_en: An expression of objection or disapproval, often manifested in a public demonstration.
cards.example_sentence: The students organized a protest against the school policy.
cards.example_es: Los estudiantes organizaron una protesta contra la política escolar.
cards.example_en: The students organized a protest against the school policy.

cards.definition_en: A public protest or a presentation that shows how something works.
cards.example_sentence: The demonstration against the labor reform attracted thousands of people.
cards.example_es: La manifestación contra la reforma laboral atrajo a miles de personas.
cards.example_en: The demonstration against the labor reform attracted thousands of people.

cards.definition_en: Activism is the practice of organized efforts to promote or oppose social, political, or environmental change.
cards.example_sentence: Youth activism has driven significant changes in environmental policy.
cards.example_es: El activismo juvenil ha impulsado cambios significativos en la política ambiental.
cards.example_en: Youth activism has driven significant changes in environmental policy.

cards.definition_en: The act of assembling and preparing troops or resources for active service.
cards.example_sentence: The mobilization of resources was key to the project's success.
cards.example_es: La movilización de los recursos fue clave para el éxito del proyecto.
cards.example_en: The mobilization of resources was key to the project's success.

cards.definition_en: the state of being informed or conscious about a particular issue among the general public
cards.example_sentence: The campaign raised public awareness about climate change.
cards.example_es: La campaña aumentó la conciencia pública sobre el cambio climático.
cards.example_en: The campaign raised public awareness about climate change.

cards.definition_en: A spoken or written account of connected events; a story.
cards.example_sentence: The narrative of the novel captures the essence of the era.
cards.example_es: La narrativa de la novela captura la esencia de la época.
cards.example_en: The narrative of the novel captures the essence of the era.

cards.definition_en: A tendency of journalists or news outlets to present information in a way that favors a particular perspective or agenda.
cards.example_sentence: Media bias affects the way we perceive the news.
cards.example_es: El sesgo mediático afecta la forma en que percibimos las noticias.
cards.example_en: Media bias affects the way we perceive the news.

cards.definition_en: The process of verifying the accuracy of information or claims.
cards.example_sentence: Fact-checking is essential to combat misinformation.
cards.example_es: La verificación de hechos es esencial para combatir la desinformación.
cards.example_en: Fact-checking is essential to combat misinformation.

cards.definition_en: To become widely known or distributed quickly.
cards.example_sentence: The rumor spread rapidly throughout the neighborhood.
cards.example_es: El rumor se difundió rápidamente por todo el vecindario.
cards.example_en: The rumor spread rapidly throughout the neighborhood.

cards.definition_en: to become widely circulated on the internet, especially through rapid sharing.
cards.example_sentence: The video went viral within hours.
cards.example_es: El video se volvió viral en cuestión de horas.
cards.example_en: The video went viral within hours.

cards.definition_en: Unintended or indirect consequences of an action or event, often negative.
cards.example_sentence: The repercussions of the decision will be felt for years.
cards.example_es: Las repercusiones de la decisión se sentirán durante años.
cards.example_en: The repercussions of the decision will be felt for years.

cards.definition_en: An outcome that was not intended or expected.
cards.example_sentence: The unintended consequence of his policy was an unexpected rise in inflation.
cards.example_es: La consecuencia imprevista de su política fue un aumento inesperado de la inflación.
cards.example_en: The unintended consequence of his policy was an unexpected rise in inflation.

cards.definition_en: The ability to recover quickly from difficulties or to adapt in the face of adversity.
collocations: resilience in the face of adversity
cards.example_sentence: The community's resilience allowed it to recover quickly after the earthquake.
cards.example_es: La resiliencia de la comunidad le permitió recuperarse rápidamente después del terremoto.
cards.example_en: The community's resilience allowed it to recover quickly after the earthquake.

cards.definition_en: The ability to adjust to new conditions or changes.
cards.example_sentence: His adaptability allowed him to overcome the changes at work.
cards.example_es: Su capacidad de adaptación le permitió superar los cambios en el trabajo.
cards.example_en: His adaptability allowed him to overcome the changes at work.

cards.definition_en: the mutual reliance between two or more entities, where each depends on the others.
cards.example_sentence: The interdependence among Latin American countries is reflected in their trade agreements.
cards.example_es: La interdependencia entre los países latinoamericanos se refleja en sus acuerdos comerciales.
cards.example_en: The interdependence among Latin American countries is reflected in their trade agreements.

cards.definition_en: General agreement among scientists about a particular scientific finding or theory.
cards.example_sentence: The scientific consensus indicates that climate change is primarily caused by human activity.
cards.example_es: El consenso científico indica que el cambio climático es causado principalmente por la actividad humana.
cards.example_en: The scientific consensus indicates that climate change is primarily caused by human activity.

cards.definition_en: A discussion that examines moral principles and values.
cards.example_sentence: The ethical debate about genetic editing is very controversial.
cards.example_es: El debate ético sobre la edición genética es muy controvertido.
cards.example_en: The ethical debate about genetic editing is very controversial.

cards.definition_en: A difference in attitudes, values, and experiences between people of different generations.
cards.example_sentence: The generation gap between young people and their parents is evident in their use of technology.
cards.example_es: La brecha generacional entre los jóvenes y sus padres se hace evidente en el uso de la tecnología.
cards.example_en: The generation gap between young people and their parents is evident in their use of technology.

cards.definition_en: The influence exerted by a group or society on an individual's behavior or decisions.
cards.example_sentence: Social pressure led him to quit his job.
cards.example_es: La presión social lo llevó a dejar su trabajo.
cards.example_en: Social pressure led him to quit his job.

cards.definition_en: The bonds, shared values, and sense of belonging that hold members of a society together.
cards.example_sentence: Social cohesion is essential for the community's development.
cards.example_es: La cohesión social es fundamental para el desarrollo de la comunidad.
cards.example_en: Social cohesion is essential for the community's development.

cards.definition_en: A feeling of being accepted and valued as part of a group or community.
cards.example_sentence: The sense of belonging he feels in his community motivates him to participate actively.
cards.example_es: El sentimiento de pertenencia que experimenta en su comunidad lo motiva a participar activamente.
cards.example_en: The sense of belonging he feels in his community motivates him to participate actively.

cards.definition_en: The process of treating a person or group as insignificant or peripheral.
cards.example_sentence: The marginalization of rural communities hinders their access to basic services.
cards.example_es: La marginalización de las comunidades rurales dificulta su acceso a servicios básicos.
cards.example_en: The marginalization of rural communities hinders their access to basic services.

cards.definition_en: The process of giving someone authority, confidence, or power to act or make decisions.
cards.example_sentence: The empowerment of women is essential for sustainable development.
cards.example_es: El empoderamiento de las mujeres es fundamental para el desarrollo sostenible.
cards.example_en: The empowerment of women is essential for sustainable development.

cards.definition_en: Climate justice is the principle of ensuring fair and equitable treatment of all people in the distribution of climate change impacts and responsibilities.
cards.example_sentence: Climate justice demands that rich countries cut their emissions to protect the most vulnerable.
cards.example_es: La justicia climática exige que los países ricos reduzcan sus emisiones para proteger a los más vulnerables.
cards.example_en: Climate justice demands that rich countries cut their emissions to protect the most vulnerable.

cards.definition_en: A principle of fairness that seeks equitable distribution of resources, rights, and opportunities within a society.
cards.example_sentence: The movement fights for social justice in the community.
cards.example_es: El movimiento lucha por la justicia social en la comunidad.
cards.example_en: The movement fights for social justice in the community.

cards.definition_en: A situation in which two or more parties are jointly accountable for an outcome.
cards.example_sentence: Shared responsibility between parents and teachers is essential for the student's success.
cards.example_es: La responsabilidad compartida entre los padres y los maestros es esencial para el éxito del estudiante.
cards.example_en: Shared responsibility between parents and teachers is essential for the student's success.

cards.definition_en: A controversial or emotionally charged topic that provokes strong opinions.
cards.example_sentence: Climate change has become a hot‑button issue in international politics.
cards.example_es: El cambio climático se ha convertido en un tema candente en la política internacional.
cards.example_en: Climate change has become a hot‑button issue in international politics.

cards.definition_en: A significant or extensive effect or influence.
cards.example_sentence: The new law will have a far-reaching impact on the country's economy.
cards.example_es: La nueva ley tendrá una amplia repercusión en la economía del país.
cards.example_en: The new law will have a far-reaching impact on the country's economy.

title: Advanced Phrasal Verbs
description: A large deck of phrasal verbs and common advanced uses to improve fluency and natural expression.

cards.definition_en: to mention or raise a topic for discussion
cards.example_sentence: During the meeting, Juan brought up the budget issue.
cards.example_es: Durante la reunión, Juan mencionó el problema del presupuesto.
cards.example_en: During the meeting, Juan brought up the budget issue.

cards.definition_en: to perform or execute a task or plan
cards.example_sentence: The committee decided to carry out the investigation next week.
cards.example_es: El comité decidió llevar a cabo la investigación la próxima semana.
cards.example_en: The committee decided to carry out the investigation next week.

cards.definition_en: to find or meet something or someone by chance
cards.example_sentence: I came across an old friend at the market.
cards.example_es: Me encontré con un viejo amigo en el mercado.
cards.example_en: I came across an old friend at the market.

cards.definition_en: To think of or produce an idea, plan, or solution.
cards.example_sentence: We need to come up with a solution before the day ends.
cards.example_es: Necesitamos idear una solución antes de que termine el día.
cards.example_en: We need to come up with a solution before the day ends.

cards.definition_en: to reduce the amount or frequency of something
cards.example_sentence: I need to cut down on sugar consumption.
cards.example_es: Necesito reducir el consumo de azúcar.
cards.example_en: I need to cut down on sugar consumption.

cards.definition_en: To eliminate or get rid of something.
collocations: do away with the old system
cards.example_sentence: The government decided to do away with import taxes.
cards.example_es: El gobierno decidió eliminar los impuestos a la importación.
cards.example_en: The government decided to do away with import taxes.

cards.definition_en: To understand or solve something by thinking about it.
cards.example_sentence: I need to figure out this problem before the meeting.
cards.example_es: Necesito resolver este problema antes de la reunión.
cards.example_en: I need to figure out this problem before the meeting.

cards.definition_en: To complete something by adding missing information.
cards.example_sentence: Please fill in the form before the deadline.
cards.example_es: Por favor, rellena el formulario antes de la fecha límite.
cards.example_en: Please fill in the form before the deadline.

cards.definition_en: To continue an action or task until it is completed.
cards.example_sentence: It's important to follow through with the projects we start.
cards.example_es: Es importante cumplir hasta el final con los proyectos que iniciamos.
cards.example_en: It's important to follow through with the projects we start.

cards.definition_en: To communicate or convey an idea, feeling, or message so that it is understood.
cards.example_sentence: The teacher managed to get the idea across clearly.
cards.example_es: El profesor logró transmitir la idea de manera clara.
cards.example_en: The teacher managed to get the idea across clearly.

cards.definition_en: To succeed in doing something that is disapproved of or illegal without being punished.
cards.example_sentence: Juan tried to rob the store, but the police caught him before he could get away with it.
cards.example_es: Juan intentó robar la tienda, pero la policía lo atrapó antes de que pudiera salirse con la suya.
cards.example_en: Juan tried to rob the store, but the police caught him before he could get away with it.

cards.definition_en: to manage to survive or cope with a situation, often with limited resources.
cards.example_sentence: With so little money, I have to get by.
cards.example_es: Con tan poco dinero, tengo que arreglármelas.
cards.example_en: With so little money, I have to get by.

cards.definition_en: To recover from or move beyond something, such as an illness, difficulty, or emotional upset.
cards.example_sentence: I had to get over my fear of flying.
cards.example_es: Tuve que superar mi miedo a volar.
cards.example_en: I had to get over my fear of flying.

cards.definition_en: to surrender or yield to someone or something
cards.example_sentence: After a long argument, he decided to give in to his demands.
cards.example_es: Después de mucho discutir, decidió ceder a sus demandas.
cards.example_en: After a long argument, he decided to give in to his demands.

cards.definition_en: To stop trying to achieve something or to surrender.
cards.example_sentence: I won't give up so easily.
cards.example_es: No voy a rendirme tan fácilmente.
cards.example_en: I won't give up so easily.

cards.definition_en: To examine or review something carefully.
cards.example_sentence: I will go over my notes before the exam.
cards.example_es: Voy a repasar mis notas antes del examen.
cards.example_en: I will go over my notes before the exam.

cards.definition_en: to develop from a child into an adult
cards.example_sentence: Children should grow up in a safe environment.
cards.example_es: Los niños deben crecer en un ambiente seguro.
cards.example_en: Children should grow up in a safe environment.

cards.definition_en: to submit something, such as a document or assignment, to the appropriate authority.
cards.example_sentence: I have to hand in the report before five.
cards.example_es: Tengo que entregar el informe antes de las cinco.
cards.example_en: I have to hand in the report before five.

cards.definition_en: To restrain or keep something from progressing or being expressed.
cards.example_sentence: You shouldn't hold back and say what you feel.
cards.example_es: No deberías reprimirte y decir lo que sientes.
cards.example_en: You shouldn't hold back and say what you feel.

cards.definition_en: To stay at the same level or pace as someone or something.
collocations: keep up with the latest trends
cards.example_sentence: It's hard to keep up with the pace of the dance class.
cards.example_es: Es difícil mantener el ritmo de la clase de baile.
cards.example_en: It's hard to keep up with the pace of the dance class.

cards.definition_en: To disappoint someone or fail to meet their expectations.
cards.example_sentence: He didn't want to let down his parents with his poor grades.
cards.example_es: No quería decepcionar a sus padres con sus malas notas.
cards.example_en: He didn't want to let down his parents with his poor grades.

cards.definition_en: to take care of someone or something
cards.example_sentence: I have to look after my little brother while my parents are away.
cards.example_es: Tengo que cuidar de mi hermano pequeño mientras mis padres están fuera.
cards.example_en: I have to look after my little brother while my parents are away.

cards.definition_en: to examine or investigate something in order to learn more about it
cards.example_sentence: I will look into the origin of the problem.
cards.example_es: Voy a investigar el origen del problema.
cards.example_en: I will look into the origin of the problem.

cards.definition_en: to regard someone with admiration and respect
collocations: look up to as an inspiration
cards.example_sentence: I have always looked up to my grandfather for his wisdom.
cards.example_es: Siempre he admirado a mi abuelo por su sabiduría.
cards.example_en: I have always looked up to my grandfather for his wisdom.

cards.definition_en: To compensate for something; to offset or counterbalance it.
cards.example_sentence: The raise makes up for the extra time he worked.
cards.example_es: El aumento de sueldo compensa el tiempo extra que trabajó.
cards.example_en: The raise makes up for the extra time he worked.

cards.definition_en: to continue or proceed to the next thing, especially after a difficulty or interruption
cards.example_sentence: After the breakup, she decided to move on with her life.
cards.example_es: Después de la ruptura, decidió seguir adelante con su vida.
cards.example_en: After the breakup, she decided to move on with her life.

cards.definition_en: To draw attention to something.
cards.example_sentence: The teacher pointed out the mistakes on the exam.
cards.example_es: El profesor señaló los errores en el examen.
cards.example_en: The teacher pointed out the mistakes on the exam.

cards.definition_en: To delay or postpone something.
cards.example_sentence: I will put off the meeting until tomorrow.
cards.example_es: Voy a posponer la reunión hasta mañana.
cards.example_en: I will put off the meeting until tomorrow.

cards.definition_en: to tolerate or endure something or someone
collocations: can't put up with his attitude
cards.example_sentence: I can't put up with the constant noise.
cards.example_es: No puedo soportar el ruido constante.
cards.example_en: I can't put up with the constant noise.

cards.definition_en: To eliminate or exclude something as a possibility.
cards.example_sentence: Doctors must rule out other causes before confirming the diagnosis.
cards.example_es: Los médicos deben descartar otras causas antes de confirmar el diagnóstico.
cards.example_en: Doctors must rule out other causes before confirming the diagnosis.

cards.definition_en: To meet or encounter someone or something unexpectedly.
cards.example_sentence: I ran into my old friend at the supermarket.
cards.example_es: Me topé con mi viejo amigo en el supermercado.
cards.example_en: I ran into my old friend at the supermarket.

cards.definition_en: To reserve something for later use or a specific purpose.
cards.example_sentence: I will set aside time to study.
cards.example_es: Voy a reservar tiempo para estudiar.
cards.example_en: I will set aside time to study.

cards.definition_en: to arrange or establish something, such as equipment, a system, or an event.
cards.example_sentence: Let's set up the tent before it starts raining.
cards.example_es: Vamos a montar la carpa antes de que empiece a llover.
cards.example_en: Let's set up the tent before it starts raining.

cards.definition_en: To organize, resolve, or clarify something.
cards.example_sentence: I need to sort out the project details before the meeting.
cards.example_es: Necesito aclarar los detalles del proyecto antes de la reunión.
cards.example_en: I need to sort out the project details before the meeting.

cards.definition_en: To speak louder or more assertively, especially to express an opinion.
cards.example_sentence: It's important to speak up when defending human rights.
cards.example_es: Es importante hablar con firmeza cuando se defienden los derechos humanos.
cards.example_en: It's important to speak up when defending human rights.

cards.definition_en: To resign from a position or office.
cards.example_sentence: The president announced that he will step down after the scandal.
cards.example_es: El presidente anunció que va a dimitir después del escándalo.
cards.example_en: The president announced that he will step down after the scandal.

cards.definition_en: to resemble or be similar to someone, especially a family member
cards.example_sentence: María takes after her mother.
cards.example_es: María se parece a su madre.
cards.example_en: María takes after her mother.

cards.definition_en: To accept responsibility for something or to assume a task or role.
cards.example_sentence: He decided to take on the responsibility of the project.
cards.example_es: Decidió asumir la responsabilidad del proyecto.
cards.example_en: He decided to take on the responsibility of the project.

cards.definition_en: to assume control or responsibility for something
cards.example_sentence: The new CEO decided to take over the company.
cards.example_es: El nuevo CEO decidió tomar el control de la empresa.
cards.example_en: The new CEO decided to take over the company.

cards.definition_en: to consider something carefully
cards.example_sentence: Before making a decision, it's important to think it over.
cards.example_es: Antes de tomar una decisión, es importante pensarlo detenidamente.
cards.example_en: Before making a decision, it's important to think it over.

cards.definition_en: To reject or refuse something.
cards.example_sentence: He decided to turn down the job offer.
cards.example_es: Decidió rechazar la oferta de trabajo.
cards.example_en: He decided to turn down the job offer.

cards.definition_en: to have a particular result or outcome
cards.example_sentence: The exam turned out to be easier than I expected.
cards.example_es: El examen resultó más fácil de lo que esperaba.
cards.example_en: The exam turned out to be easier than I expected.

cards.definition_en: to succeed or have a favorable outcome
cards.example_sentence: In the end, everything worked out.
cards.example_es: Al final, todo se salió bien.
cards.example_en: In the end, everything worked out.

cards.definition_en: to withdraw from an agreement, plan, or commitment
collocations: back out at the last minute
cards.example_sentence: He decided to back out of the project before it was too late.
cards.example_es: Decidió retirarse del proyecto antes de que fuera demasiado tarde.
cards.example_en: He decided to back out of the project before it was too late.

cards.definition_en: To end a relationship with someone.
cards.example_sentence: He decided to break off his relationship with his girlfriend after three years.
cards.example_es: Decidió romper con su novia después de tres años.
cards.example_en: He decided to break off his relationship with his girlfriend after three years.

cards.definition_en: To succeed in overcoming an obstacle or to make a way forward.
cards.example_sentence: The pioneers had to break through the jungle.
cards.example_es: Los pioneros tuvieron que abrirse paso en la selva.
cards.example_en: The pioneers had to break through the jungle.

cards.definition_en: To interrupt someone while they are speaking.
cards.example_sentence: You shouldn't cut in when someone is speaking.
cards.example_es: No deberías interrumpir cuando alguien está hablando.
cards.example_en: You shouldn't cut in when someone is speaking.

cards.definition_en: To consume something completely until none remains.
cards.example_sentence: The fire managed to use up all the water supply in the area.
cards.example_es: El incendio logró agotar todo el suministro de agua de la zona.
cards.example_en: The fire managed to use up all the water supply in the area.

cards.definition_en: to finally reach a particular place, condition, or result, often unexpectedly
cards.example_sentence: If you don't study, you could end up on the street.
cards.example_es: Si no estudias, podrías terminar en la calle.
cards.example_en: If you don't study, you could end up on the street.

cards.definition_en: To discover or learn something.
cards.example_sentence: After searching for hours, I finally found out the solution to the problem.
cards.example_es: Después de buscar durante horas, finalmente di con la solución al problema.
cards.example_en: After searching for hours, I finally found out the solution to the problem.

cards.definition_en: To gradually disappear or lose intensity.
cards.example_sentence: Over time, the lantern's light began to fade away.
cards.example_es: Con el tiempo, la luz del farol se fue apagando.
cards.example_en: Over time, the lantern's light began to fade away.

cards.definition_en: to take strict or severe measures against something or someone
cards.example_sentence: The government decided to crack down on tax evasion.
cards.example_es: El gobierno decidió aplastar la evasión fiscal.
cards.example_en: The government decided to crack down on tax evasion.

cards.definition_en: To dismiss or reject something or someone in a casual or indifferent manner.
cards.example_sentence: When they proposed the idea to him, he brushed it off.
cards.example_es: Cuando le propusieron la idea, la rechazó de plano.
cards.example_en: When they proposed the idea to him, he brushed it off.

cards.definition_en: to agree with or support something or someone
cards.example_sentence: He decided to go along with his brother on the hike.
cards.example_es: Decidió acompañar a su hermano en la caminata.
cards.example_en: He decided to go along with his brother on the hike.

cards.definition_en: to tolerate or endure something or someone
cards.example_sentence: I can't put up with the constant construction noise.
cards.example_es: No puedo tolerar el ruido constante de la construcción.
cards.example_en: I can't put up with the constant construction noise.

cards.definition_en: To intervene or become involved in a situation.
collocations: step in at the last minute
cards.example_sentence: The doctor had to step in quickly to save the patient.
cards.example_es: El médico tuvo que intervenir rápidamente para salvar al paciente.
cards.example_en: The doctor had to step in quickly to save the patient.

cards.definition_en: To gradually stop using or providing something.
cards.example_sentence: The company decided to phase out its mobile phone market.
cards.example_es: La empresa decidió retirarse gradualmente del mercado de teléfonos móviles.
cards.example_en: The company decided to phase out its mobile phone market.

cards.definition_en: To introduce something gradually or in stages.
cards.example_sentence: The company decided to phase in the new software to avoid problems.
cards.example_es: La empresa decidió introducir gradualmente el nuevo software para evitar problemas.
cards.example_en: The company decided to phase in the new software to avoid problems.

cards.definition_en: to cause something to collapse, fail, or separate into its component parts.
cards.example_sentence: The car started to break down and stopped in the middle of the road.
cards.example_es: El coche empezó a fallar y se detuvo en medio de la carretera.
cards.example_en: The car started to break down and stopped in the middle of the road.

cards.definition_en: To end a romantic relationship.
cards.example_sentence: After five years together, they decided to break up.
cards.example_es: Después de cinco años de relación, decidieron separarse.
cards.example_en: After five years together, they decided to break up.

cards.definition_en: to become or cause someone to become less agitated or upset
collocations: Calm down, it's not a big deal
cards.example_sentence: After the argument, John decided to calm down and take a deep breath.
cards.example_es: Después de la discusión, Juan decidió calmarse y respirar profundamente.
cards.example_en: After the argument, John decided to calm down and take a deep breath.

cards.definition_en: To cancel or stop something that was planned.
cards.example_sentence: We decided to call off the party.
cards.example_es: Decidimos cancelar la fiesta.
cards.example_en: We decided to call off the party.

cards.definition_en: to continue doing something
cards.example_sentence: He decided to carry on with the project despite the obstacles.
cards.example_es: Decidió seguir adelante con el proyecto a pesar de los obstáculos.
cards.example_en: He decided to carry on with the project despite the obstacles.

cards.definition_en: to reach the same level or position as someone or something after falling behind
cards.example_sentence: I need to catch up with my classmates so I don't fall behind.
cards.example_es: Necesito alcanzar a mis compañeros de clase para no quedarme atrás.
cards.example_en: I need to catch up with my classmates so I don't fall behind.

cards.definition_en: to examine or look at something, or to borrow it from a library
cards.example_sentence: I will check out the train schedule before leaving.
cards.example_es: Voy a comprobar el horario del tren antes de salir.
cards.example_en: I will check out the train schedule before leaving.

cards.definition_en: to rely on someone or something
cards.example_sentence: You can count on my help to finish the project.
cards.example_es: Puedes contar con mi ayuda para terminar el proyecto.
cards.example_en: You can count on my help to finish the project.

cards.definition_en: To stop or interrupt something abruptly.
cards.example_sentence: The host decided to cut off the interview abruptly when the guest became nervous.
cards.example_es: El presentador decidió interrumpir abruptamente la entrevista cuando el invitado se puso nervioso.
cards.example_en: The host decided to cut off the interview abruptly when the guest became nervous.

cards.definition_en: To address, manage, or handle a problem, situation, or person.
cards.example_sentence: I have to deal with many problems at work.
cards.example_es: Tengo que lidiar con muchos problemas en el trabajo.
cards.example_en: I have to deal with many problems at work.

cards.definition_en: To manage or survive without something.
cards.example_sentence: I'm going to do without sugar.
cards.example_es: Voy a acabar con el azúcar.
cards.example_en: I'm going to do without sugar.

cards.definition_en: To quit a school, program, or activity before completing it.
cards.example_sentence: He decided to drop out of college after the first semester.
cards.example_es: Decidió abandonar la universidad después del primer semestre.
cards.example_en: He decided to drop out of college after the first semester.

cards.definition_en: to consume something completely, especially to finish eating it
cards.example_sentence: I'm going to eat up the whole cake.
cards.example_es: Voy a comerme todo el pastel.
cards.example_en: I'm going to eat up the whole cake.

cards.definition_en: To dismiss or rationalize something as unimportant or untrue by offering an explanation that reduces its significance.
cards.example_sentence: He tried to explain away his mistake as if it were nothing.
cards.example_es: Trató de explicar su error como si no fuera gran cosa.
cards.example_en: He tried to explain away his mistake as if it were nothing.

cards.definition_en: To cause something to become full or to become full oneself.
cards.example_sentence: The glass filled up with water.
cards.example_es: El vaso se llenó de agua.
cards.example_en: The glass filled up with water.

cards.definition_en: to be accepted as part of a group or environment
cards.example_sentence: I had a hard time fitting in with the new work team.
cards.example_es: Me costó mucho encajar en el nuevo equipo de trabajo.
cards.example_en: I had a hard time fitting in with the new work team.

cards.definition_en: to continue doing something despite obstacles or difficulties
cards.example_sentence: We must get on with the project despite the setbacks.
cards.example_es: Debemos seguir adelante a pesar de los contratiempos.
cards.example_en: We must get on with the project despite the setbacks.

cards.definition_en: To descend from or leave a vehicle, platform, or other surface.
cards.example_sentence: I got off the bus just in time.
cards.example_es: Me bajé del autobús justo a tiempo.
cards.example_en: I got off the bus just in time.

cards.definition_en: To board or mount something, such as a vehicle or animal.
cards.example_sentence: I'm going to get on the bus before it leaves.
cards.example_es: Voy a subirme al autobús antes de que salga.
cards.example_en: I'm going to get on the bus before it leaves.

cards.definition_en: To return to a previous place or condition.
collocations: go back to the drawing board
cards.example_sentence: I need to go back home before the storm.
cards.example_es: Necesito volver a casa antes de la tormenta.
cards.example_en: I need to go back home before the storm.

cards.definition_en: To leave a place and go outside.
cards.example_sentence: We are going to go out after dinner.
cards.example_es: Vamos a salir después de la cena.
cards.example_en: We are going to go out after dinner.

cards.definition_en: A command telling someone to act more quickly.
cards.example_sentence: You have to hurry up if you don't want to miss the train.
cards.example_es: Debes acelerarte si no quieres perder el tren.
cards.example_en: You have to hurry up if you don't want to miss the train.

cards.definition_en: To delay or postpone something.
cards.example_sentence: We decided to hold off the meeting until later.
cards.example_es: Decidimos posponer la reunión hasta más tarde.
cards.example_en: We decided to hold off the meeting until later.

cards.definition_en: To continue doing something or maintain a certain level or pace.
cards.example_sentence: I need to keep up to finish the project.
cards.example_es: Necesito permanecer despierto para terminar el proyecto.
cards.example_en: I need to keep up to finish the project.

cards.definition_en: To leave hurriedly, especially after stealing something.
cards.example_sentence: The customers decided to make off without paying the restaurant bill.
cards.example_es: Los clientes decidieron irse sin pagar la cuenta del restaurante.
cards.example_en: The customers decided to make off without paying the restaurant bill.

cards.definition_en: To create something false or fictitious, such as a story, excuse, or invention.
cards.example_sentence: She decided to make up an excuse to be late.
cards.example_es: Ella decidió inventar una excusa para llegar tarde.
cards.example_en: She decided to make up an excuse to be late.

cards.definition_en: To go beyond or ignore something.
cards.example_sentence: You can't move past the rules of the game.
cards.example_es: No puedes pasar por encima de las reglas del juego.
cards.example_en: You can't move past the rules of the game.

cards.definition_en: to give or transmit something to someone else
collocations: pass on to the next generation
cards.example_sentence: He passed on that the meeting had been cancelled.
cards.example_es: Mencionó casualmente que la reunión se había cancelado.
cards.example_en: He passed on that the meeting had been cancelled.

cards.definition_en: To lift something from a surface or to collect it.
collocations: pick up where you left off
cards.example_sentence: I will pick up the package from the office.
cards.example_es: Voy a recoger el paquete de la oficina.
cards.example_en: I will pick up the package from the office.

cards.definition_en: To identify or locate something precisely, often by determining its exact nature or details.
cards.example_sentence: The detective managed to pin down the location of the body.
cards.example_es: El detective logró señalar con precisión la ubicación del cuerpo.
cards.example_en: The detective managed to pin down the location of the body.

cards.definition_en: to propose or suggest something for consideration
cards.example_sentence: The lawyer put forward the evidence to the jury.
cards.example_es: El abogado presentó formalmente la evidencia al jurado.
cards.example_en: The lawyer put forward the evidence to the jury.

cards.definition_en: To dress oneself in something, or to cause something to be worn.
cards.example_sentence: I'm going to put on the heating because it's cold.
cards.example_es: Voy a encender la calefacción porque hace frío.
cards.example_en: I'm going to put on the heating because it's cold.

cards.definition_en: To extinguish a fire, light, or similar source.
cards.example_sentence: Put out the candle before you leave.
cards.example_es: Apaga la vela antes de irte.
cards.example_en: Put out the candle before you leave.

cards.definition_en: To discard or eject something.
cards.example_sentence: The guard had to throw out the thief from the building.
cards.example_es: El guardia tuvo que expulsar al ladrón del edificio.
cards.example_en: The guard had to throw out the thief from the building.

cards.definition_en: to appear or occur unexpectedly
cards.example_sentence: Old problems can crop up after years.
cards.example_es: Los problemas antiguos pueden resurgir después de años.
cards.example_en: Old problems can crop up after years.

cards.definition_en: to become aware of something
cards.example_sentence: I cottoned on that I had left the door open.
cards.example_es: Me di cuenta de que había dejado la puerta abierta.
cards.example_en: I cottoned on that I had left the door open.

cards.definition_en: to confront or deal with something directly, especially a problem or difficulty.
cards.example_sentence: I have to face up to my fears.
cards.example_es: Tengo que enfrentarme a mis miedos.
cards.example_en: I have to face up to my fears.

cards.definition_en: to write something quickly, especially brief notes.
cards.example_sentence: During the class, I like to jot down notes so I don't forget anything.
cards.example_es: Durante la clase, me gusta tomar apuntes para no olvidar nada.
cards.example_en: During the class, I like to jot down notes so I don't forget anything.

cards.definition_en: To simplify something to its essential point or main idea.
collocations: In the end, it boils down to
cards.example_sentence: The whole problem boils down to a matter of trust.
cards.example_es: Todo el problema se reduce a una cuestión de confianza.
cards.example_en: The whole problem boils down to a matter of trust.

cards.definition_en: To imagine or invent something, especially an idea or plan.
cards.example_sentence: The scientist dreamed up a brilliant idea to solve the problem.
cards.example_es: El científico salió con una idea brillante para resolver el problema.
cards.example_en: The scientist dreamed up a brilliant idea to solve the problem.

cards.definition_en: to leave or abandon something or someone
cards.example_sentence: He decided to walk away from his job after ten years.
cards.example_es: Decidió renunciar a su trabajo después de diez años.
cards.example_en: He decided to walk away from his job after ten years.

cards.definition_en: to become comfortable and stable in a place or situation, often by establishing a residence or routine
collocations: settle down in a new city
cards.example_sentence: After moving to the new city, he decided to settle down in a small apartment near the park.
cards.example_es: Después de mudarse a la nueva ciudad, decidió asentarse en un pequeño apartamento cerca del parque.
cards.example_en: After moving to the new city, he decided to settle down in a small apartment near the park.

cards.definition_en: to originate or develop from something
cards.example_sentence: The problem stems from poor communication.
cards.example_es: El problema deriva de una mala comunicación.
cards.example_en: The problem stems from poor communication.

cards.definition_en: to assume a higher level of responsibility or authority
cards.example_sentence: When the captain fell ill, he had to step up and take command of the ship.
cards.example_es: Cuando el capitán cayó enfermo, tuvo que asumir el mando del barco.
cards.example_en: When the captain fell ill, he had to step up and take command of the ship.

cards.definition_en: To gradually remove or eliminate unwanted or undesirable items or people.
cards.example_sentence: The company decided to weed out unnecessary positions.
cards.example_es: La empresa decidió suprimir gradualmente los puestos de trabajo innecesarios.
cards.example_en: The company decided to weed out unnecessary positions.

cards.definition_en: to separate something into smaller parts or components
cards.example_sentence: Break down the information into smaller parts to understand it better.
cards.example_es: Desmenuzar la información en partes más pequeñas ayuda a comprenderla mejor.
cards.example_en: Break down the information into smaller parts to understand it better.

cards.definition_en: to survive or recover from a difficult situation
cards.example_sentence: With a lot of effort, we managed to pull through the economic crisis.
cards.example_es: Con mucho trabajo, logramos superar con esfuerzo la crisis económica.
cards.example_en: With a lot of effort, we managed to pull through the economic crisis.

cards.definition_en: to withdraw from a commitment or fail to follow through with a plan
cards.example_sentence: I backed out of the meeting because I was feeling sick.
cards.example_es: No me presenté a la reunión porque me sentía enfermo.
cards.example_en: I backed out of the meeting because I was feeling sick.

cards.definition_en: To draw a line through text or a mark to indicate it should be deleted or ignored.
cards.example_sentence: Please cross out the mistakes in the document.
cards.example_es: Por favor, tachar los errores en el documento.
cards.example_en: Please cross out the mistakes in the document.

cards.definition_en: to accompany someone or something
cards.example_sentence: I go together with my dog to the park.
cards.example_es: Voy al parque acompañado de mi perro.
cards.example_en: I go together with my dog to the park.

cards.definition_en: to rely again on something or someone for support or help
cards.example_sentence: When I don't know what to do, I always fall back on my old habits.
cards.example_es: Cuando no sé qué hacer, siempre recaigo en mis viejos hábitos.
cards.example_en: When I don't know what to do, I always fall back on my old habits.

cards.definition_en: To continue doing something with determination despite obstacles.
cards.example_sentence: He pressed ahead with finishing the project on time.
cards.example_es: Insistió en terminar el proyecto a tiempo.
cards.example_en: He pressed ahead with finishing the project on time.

title: Discussion and Debate
description: Expressions for giving opinions, agreeing, disagreeing, clarifying, and structuring advanced spoken English.

cards.definition_en: A statement indicating total agreement with something.
cards.example_sentence: I completely agree with your proposal.
cards.example_es: Estoy totalmente de acuerdo con tu propuesta.
cards.example_en: I completely agree with your proposal.

cards.spanish: Estoy de acuerdo hasta cierto punto
cards.definition_en: To express partial agreement with something.
collocations: I agree to some extent with you
I agree to some extent, however
I agree to some extent on that point
cards.example_sentence: I agree to some extent with what you say.
cards.example_es: Estoy de acuerdo hasta cierto punto con lo que dices.
cards.example_en: I agree to some extent with what you say.

cards.english: I do not see it that way
cards.definition_en: A statement indicating that the speaker does not share that perspective.
main_translations_es: No lo percibo de esa manera
collocations: I do not see it that way, but...
I do not see it that way at all
I do not see it that way, however
cards.example_sentence: I don't see it that way, but I understand your point.
cards.example_es: No lo veo así, pero entiendo tu punto.
cards.example_en: I don't see it that way, but I understand your point.

cards.definition_en: A statement indicating that someone is not fully persuaded.
collocations: I am not entirely convinced that…
We are not entirely convinced about…
cards.example_sentence: I am not entirely convinced that this is the best solution.
cards.example_es: No estoy del todo convencido de que esa sea la mejor solución.
cards.example_en: I am not entirely convinced that this is the best solution.

cards.definition_en: A polite way of saying that you disagree.
collocations: I beg to differ with you
I beg to differ on that point
cards.example_sentence: I beg to differ with his opinion on the matter.
cards.example_es: Discrepo con su opinión sobre el tema.
cards.example_en: I beg to differ with his opinion on the matter.

cards.spanish: Eso no refleja toda la realidad
cards.english: That does not tell the whole story
cards.definition_en: It means that something fails to convey the complete truth or full situation.
main_translations_es: Eso no muestra toda la realidad
Eso no cuenta toda la historia
Eso no refleja todo el panorama
collocations: The numbers do not tell the whole story
One example does not tell the whole story
Statistics often do not tell the whole story
A single incident does not tell the whole story
cards.example_sentence: That does not tell the whole story about the situation.
cards.example_es: Eso no refleja toda la realidad de la situación.
cards.example_en: That does not tell the whole story about the situation.

cards.definition_en: A phrase used to indicate that something is being considered according to one's own point of view.
collocations: From my perspective, the situation is clear
From my perspective, we should proceed
From my perspective, the results are promising
cards.example_sentence: From my perspective, education should be free for everyone.
cards.example_es: Desde mi punto de vista, la educación debería ser gratuita para todos.
cards.example_en: From my perspective, education should be free for everyone.

cards.definition_en: A phrase used to introduce one's personal opinion or perspective.
collocations: as I see it, the problem is
as I see it, we should
as I see it, that's the case
cards.example_sentence: As I see it, the situation is unsustainable.
cards.example_es: Tal como yo lo veo, la situación es insostenible.
cards.example_en: As I see it, the situation is unsustainable.

cards.definition_en: It indicates that the speaker is stating something based on their current understanding.
collocations: as far as I understand it
as far as I understand the situation
as far as I understand from the report
cards.example_sentence: As far as I understand, the project will be completed on time.
cards.example_es: Por lo que entiendo, el proyecto se completará a tiempo.
cards.example_en: As far as I understand, the project will be completed on time.

cards.definition_en: It means that the speaker is stating something based on their current knowledge, but may not be certain.
collocations: as far as I know, it's true
as far as I know, there are no issues
as far as I know, that's the case
cards.example_sentence: As far as I know, the meeting will be tomorrow at ten.
cards.example_es: Hasta donde sé, la reunión será mañana a las diez.
cards.example_en: As far as I know, the meeting will be tomorrow at ten.

cards.definition_en: A phrase used to introduce a statement that qualifies or contrasts with what was previously said.
collocations: That said, the results are inconclusive
cards.example_sentence: That said, I think we should proceed with the original plan.
cards.example_es: Dicho esto, creo que deberíamos continuar con el plan original.
cards.example_en: That said, I think we should proceed with the original plan.

cards.definition_en: Indicates that two or more events occur simultaneously.
cards.example_sentence: At the same time, we decided to go to the beach and visit my grandparents.
cards.example_es: Al mismo tiempo, decidimos viajar a la playa y visitar a mis abuelos.
cards.example_en: At the same time, we decided to go to the beach and visit my grandparents.

cards.definition_en: In a way that does not diminish or lessen the importance of something.
cards.example_sentence: Without downplaying the minor issues, we must focus on the main solution.
cards.example_es: Sin restarle importancia a los problemas menores, debemos enfocarnos en la solución principal.
cards.example_en: Without downplaying the minor issues, we must focus on the main solution.

cards.definition_en: A phrase used to restate something in a different way.
cards.example_sentence: In other words, the project is delayed.
cards.example_es: En otras palabras, el proyecto está retrasado.
cards.example_en: In other words, the project is delayed.

cards.definition_en: Used to introduce a restatement of something in other words.
cards.example_sentence: Put differently, the situation is unsustainable.
cards.example_es: Dicho de otro modo, la situación es insostenible.
cards.example_en: Put differently, the situation is unsustainable.

cards.definition_en: Used to introduce a more exact statement or clarification.
collocations: To be more precise, the answer is ...
To be more precise, we should note that ...
cards.example_sentence: To be more precise, the project will be completed in three months.
cards.example_es: Para ser más preciso, el proyecto se completará en tres meses.
cards.example_en: To be more precise, the project will be completed in three months.

cards.definition_en: A polite way of saying that you will restate something using different words.
collocations: Let me rephrase that for clarity
Let me rephrase that in simpler terms
Let me rephrase that to be sure
Let me rephrase that another way
cards.example_sentence: Let me rephrase that to make it clearer.
cards.example_es: Permíteme reformularlo para que sea más claro.
cards.example_en: Let me rephrase that to make it clearer.

cards.english: What I am trying to say is
cards.definition_en: A phrase used to introduce or clarify what the speaker intends to convey.
collocations: What I am trying to say is that...
What I am trying to say is, in short,...
What I am trying to say is, basically,...
cards.example_sentence: What I am trying to say is that we need more time.
cards.example_es: Lo que intento decir es que necesitamos más tiempo.
cards.example_en: What I am trying to say is that we need more time.

cards.definition_en: A phrase used to introduce a detailed analysis or explanation of something.
collocations: If we break it down further
If we break it down step by step
If we break it down, we can see
If we break it down, the problem becomes clear
cards.example_sentence: If we break it down carefully, we will find the cause of the problem.
cards.example_es: Si lo analizamos detenidamente, encontraremos la causa del problema.
cards.example_en: If we break it down carefully, we will find the cause of the problem.

cards.definition_en: A statement acknowledging that someone has made a valid or reasonable argument.
collocations: I have to admit that's a fair point.
cards.example_sentence: That is a fair point, we should consider it before deciding.
cards.example_es: Ese es un buen punto, deberíamos considerarlo antes de decidir.
cards.example_en: That is a fair point, we should consider it before deciding.

cards.definition_en: It means something deserves attention or consideration.
cards.example_sentence: That is worth considering, so we should review it.
cards.example_es: Eso merece atención, así que deberíamos revisarlo.
cards.example_en: That is worth considering, so we should review it.

cards.english: I had not thought of that
cards.definition_en: A statement indicating that one had not considered something before.
collocations: I had not thought of that before
I had not thought of that possibility
I had not thought of that angle
